  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this production schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  crew_size_required varchar [note: 'Number of operators or crew members required to execute this production schedule entry. Used for labor capacity planning.']
  execution_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the actual production execution for this schedule entry was completed and confirmed by the MES. Used for schedule vs. actual performance analysis.']
  forecast_demand_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The forecasted demand quantity that this schedule entry is intended to fulfill. Used for demand-driven planning and forecast accuracy tracking.']
  frozen_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this schedule entry falls within the frozen planning horizon where changes are restricted to maintain schedule stability.']
  haccp_ccp_applicable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this production schedule entry involves HACCP Critical Control Points that require monitoring and documentation per food safety regulations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this production schedule record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_replanning_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this schedule entry was last revised or replanned. Used for tracking schedule stability and replanning frequency.']
  lot_number_planned varchar [note: 'Pre-assigned lot number for traceability purposes. Enables First In First Out (FIFO) and First Expired First Out (FEFO) inventory consumption tracking and lot genealogy.']
  material_availability_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether all required raw materials and ingredients are available for this scheduled production. Confirmed indicates full availability; partial indicates some materials available; pending indicates materials not yet checked; shortage indicates insufficient inventory.']
  otif_target_date date [note: 'The target delivery date for On Time In Full (OTIF) performance measurement. Used to track schedule adherence and customer service level.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU planned to be produced in this schedule entry, expressed in the base unit of measure for the SKU.']
  planned_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the planned quantity (e.g., cases, kilograms, liters, pallets). Aligns with SKU base UOM.']
  planning_horizon varchar [note: 'The planning time bucket or horizon this schedule belongs to (e.g., weekly, daily, shift-level). Indicates the granularity of the planning cycle.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this schedule entry. Critical indicates customer-committed or regulatory deadline; high indicates tight inventory; normal indicates standard planning; low indicates buffer or make-to-stock.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this production run requires in-process or post-production quality inspection per Quality Management (QM) protocols.']
  released_to_mes_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this schedule entry was released to the Manufacturing Execution System (MES) for execution. Marks the transition from planning to execution.']
  replanning_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the most recent replanning event (e.g., demand change, material shortage, equipment breakdown, customer request). Used for root cause analysis of schedule instability.']
  safety_stock_target decimal(18,3) [note: 'Target safety stock level that this production schedule entry is intended to maintain or replenish. Used for inventory optimization.']
  schedule_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments from the planner regarding special considerations, constraints, or instructions for this schedule entry.']
  schedule_source varchar [note: 'Origin system or method that generated this schedule entry. MRP indicates Material Requirements Planning; manual indicates planner override; IBP indicates Integrated Business Planning system; demand_sensing indicates real-time demand signal; optimization indicates algorithmic sequencing.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production schedule entry. Draft indicates preliminary planning; firmed indicates committed plan; released indicates sent to MES for execution; executed indicates production completed; cancelled indicates schedule entry voided.']
  schedule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the production schedule. Allows tracking of schedule revisions and replanning iterations (e.g., V1, V2, V2.1).']
  scheduled_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total planned production duration in minutes, including run time but excluding changeover time. Used for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) baseline calculations.']
  scheduled_end_datetime timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time when production for this schedule entry is expected to be completed. Used for capacity planning and On Time In Full (OTIF) tracking.']
  scheduled_start_datetime timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time when production for this schedule entry is expected to begin. Used for capacity planning and sequencing.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'The order position of this schedule entry within the work center\'s production sequence for the planning period. Used for sequencing optimization and changeover minimization.']
  setup_instructions varchar [note: 'Special setup or configuration instructions for the work center to execute this schedule entry. May reference standard operating procedures (SOPs) or work instructions.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the production shift during which this schedule entry is planned (e.g., Day, Night, Swing). Used for labor planning and shift performance analysis.']

  Note: 'Forward-looking production schedule records generated by the planning system (SAP IBP / JDA Blue Yonder) and executed via MES. Captures schedule version, planning horizon, scheduled production order reference, work center assignment, SKU, planned quantity, scheduled start datetime, scheduled end datetime, schedule status (draft, firmed, released, executed, cancelled), schedule source (MRP, manual, IBP), changeover time allocated, sequence number on line, and last replanning timestamp. Enables sequencing optimization, changeover minimization, and OTIF performance tracking.'
}

Table manufacturing.haccp_ccp_log {
  haccp_ccp_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the HACCP critical control point monitoring log entry. Primary key for the log record.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: A HACCP CCP log entry is recorded during the execution of a specific production batch. Adding batch_record_id as a FK to batch_record normalizes the relationship and replaces the denormalized batch_nu']
  critical_control_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.critical_control_point. Business justification: A CCP log entry records a monitoring event at a specific CCP. Linking to quality.critical_control_point identifies which CCP (with its defined limits and hazard type) was monitored — the core HACCP co']
  equipment_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific equipment or instrument used to perform the CCP monitoring (e.g., thermometer serial number, metal detector unit ID). Critical for calibration traceability.']
  food_safety_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.food_safety_plan. Business justification: Each CCP monitoring log entry is governed by a specific food safety plan defining critical limits and monitoring procedures. FSMA and HACCP regulations require CCP records to reference the controlling']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: HACCP CCP log measurements (temperature, pH, pressure) must be validated against formulation-specified critical limits (pasteurization_temperature, target_ph_min/max). Regulatory audits require tracin']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: FSMA/FDA requires CCP monitoring records be traceable to the governing HACCP plan. haccp_ccp_log records measured values at CCPs; the HACCP plan defines the critical limits and monitoring requirements']
  ingredient_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ingredient.lot. Business justification: FSMA Section 204 and HACCP regulations require CCP monitoring records to be traceable to the specific ingredient lot processed at each control point. Enables precise recall scope determination and reg']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order under which this CCP monitoring occurred. Links the CCP log to the manufacturing execution context.']
  quarantine_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.quarantine_hold. Business justification: When a HACCP CCP log records a critical limit deviation (regulatory_hold_triggered_flag=true), a quarantine hold is immediately placed on affected inventory. Food safety regulations require direct tra']
  raw_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ingredient.raw_material. Business justification: HACCP plans are ingredient-specific; CCP monitoring records must be traceable to the raw material being processed at each control point. FSMA and HACCP regulatory requirements mandate this link for fo']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center or production line where the CCP monitoring took place. Enables location-specific analysis of control point performance.']
  ccp_identifier varchar [note: 'The unique code or identifier for the critical control point as defined in the HACCP plan (e.g., CCP-1, CCP-2-PASTEURIZE).']
  ccp_name varchar [note: 'The descriptive name of the critical control point being monitored (e.g., Pasteurization Temperature, Metal Detection, pH Check, Brix Measurement, Allergen Verification).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or context related to the CCP monitoring event. May include operator observations, environmental conditions, or other relevant details.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether a corrective action is required based on the CCP monitoring result (True = corrective action needed, False = no action needed).']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action(s) taken in response to a CCP deviation. Documents the specific steps taken to bring the process back into control and disposition of affected product.']
  critical_limit_lower decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum acceptable value for this CCP as defined in the HACCP plan. Values below this threshold indicate a deviation requiring corrective action.']
  critical_limit_upper decimal(18,4) [note: 'The maximum acceptable value for this CCP as defined in the HACCP plan. Values above this threshold indicate a deviation requiring corrective action.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'The origin of the CCP monitoring data (mes_automated = automatically captured from Aveva/Wonderware MES, manual_entry = operator-entered, laboratory_system = from LIMS, sensor_network = from IoT sensors).']
  deviation_severity varchar [note: 'Classification of the severity of the CCP deviation if one occurred (minor = slight deviation with low risk, major = significant deviation requiring investigation, critical = severe deviation with immediate food safety risk).']
  hazard_type varchar [note: 'The category of food safety hazard that this CCP is designed to control (biological, chemical, physical, or allergen).']
  in_limit_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the measured value falls within the defined critical limits (True = within limits, False = out of specification).']
  measured_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual measured or observed value at the critical control point (e.g., temperature reading, pH level, Brix degree, metal detector sensitivity test result).']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'The required frequency of monitoring for this CCP as defined in the HACCP plan (e.g., continuous, every 15 minutes, every batch, hourly).']
  monitoring_method varchar [note: 'The method or procedure used to monitor this CCP (e.g., calibrated thermometer, pH meter, metal detector test, visual inspection, laboratory analysis).']
  monitoring_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the CCP measurement or observation was recorded. Critical for establishing real-time compliance and traceability.']
  product_disposition varchar [note: 'The final disposition decision for product affected by this CCP monitoring event (released = approved for distribution, rework = reprocessed to meet specifications, reprocess = returned to earlier production step, destroyed = disposed of, quarantine = held pending investigation).']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this CCP log record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data integrity and compliance.']
  record_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this CCP log record was last modified. Critical for audit trail and data integrity compliance.']
  regulatory_hold_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this CCP deviation triggered a regulatory hold on the batch, preventing release until investigation and disposition are complete (True = hold triggered, False = no hold).']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the supervisor or quality manager completed their review of this CCP log entry.']
  supervisor_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this CCP log entry requires supervisor or quality manager review due to deviation or other exceptional circumstances (True = review required, False = no review needed).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the measured value is expressed (e.g., Celsius, Fahrenheit, pH units, Brix degrees, ppm, CFU/g).']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the CCP monitoring record was verified and signed off by the responsible operator or quality personnel.']

  Note: 'Real-time and operator-entered log of HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) critical control point (CCP) monitoring records for each production batch. Captures CCP identifier, CCP name (e.g., pasteurization temperature, metal detection, pH check, Brix measurement), production order, batch number, work center, monitoring timestamp, measured value, unit of measure, critical limit lower bound, critical limit upper bound, in-limit flag, corrective action required flag, corrective action taken description, verified by operator ID, and regulatory hold triggered flag. Mandatory for FDA FSMA compliance and GFSI certification. Sourced from Aveva/Wonderware MES and manual entry. SSOT boundary: This entity owns real-time CCP monitoring readings and corrective actions during production execution. The quality domain owns the HACCP plan definition, hazard analysis, and CCP validation/verification programs.'
}

Table manufacturing.lot_consumption {
  lot_consumption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lot consumption transaction record. Primary key for the lot consumption entity.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the batch record documenting the production run. Links lot consumption to the specific batch manufacturing execution record for GMP compliance and traceability.']
  bom_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bom_line. Business justification: Lot consumption records track which ingredient lots were consumed against specific BOM line items. FSMA 204 traceability requires linking actual consumed lots to the BOM component they satisfy. BOM li']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Needed for Material Consumption Costing and variance analysis; cost_center_code is a denormalized code that should reference finance.cost_center.']
  inbound_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_receipt. Business justification: FSMA one-up/one-back traceability requires linking material consumption records to the specific goods receipt document. lot_consumption → inbound_receipt enables precise recall scope determination, FE']
  ingredient_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ingredient.lot. Business justification: REQUIRED: Enables lot‑level traceability for recalls and FDA FSMA reporting by linking each consumption record to the specific ingredient lot used.']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_trace. Business justification: FSMA one-up-one-down traceability requires linking ingredient lot consumption events to the inventory lot trace record of the consumed ingredient lot. Recall investigations must identify which product']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Lot consumption records occur at a plant; linking to plant provides authoritative plant data and removes the free‑text plant_code.']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: Linking consumption to a specific PO line enables variance analysis and accurate spend tracking.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order against which this lot consumption occurred. Links consumption to the manufacturing order that triggered the material usage.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Material consumption is allocated to specific promotions for accurate cost‑of‑goods‑sold and promotional spend reconciliation.']
  recall_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.recall_event. Business justification: During a recall, ingredient lot consumption records identify which raw material lots entered affected finished goods batches. FSMA traceability rules require forward and backward lot tracing — linking']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: REQUIRED: Links each consumption event to the exact inventory lot consumed, supporting lot traceability, regulatory reporting, and OTIF calculations.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Identifying the supplier of each consumed lot supports quality audits and allergen compliance.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center or production line where the material was consumed. Links consumption to the specific manufacturing resource.']
  allergen_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the consumed material contains or may contain allergens as defined by FDA and FALCPA regulations. Used for allergen control and cross-contamination prevention.']
  backflush_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this consumption was automatically backflushed based on production confirmation (True) or manually posted (False). Backflushing automates material consumption based on BOM and production output.']
  bom_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The planned or standard quantity of this material specified in the Bill of Materials for the production order. Used to calculate consumption variance.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the consumption transaction in the system workflow: confirmed (posted to inventory), pending (awaiting approval), rejected (not accepted), or reversed (cancelled after posting).']
  consumption_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The quantity of material consumed from the specified lot during production. Recorded in the unit of measure specified in the unit_of_measure field.']
  consumption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the material lot was consumed in the production process. Critical for FIFO/FEFO compliance and production timeline tracking.']
  consumption_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consumption event indicating the purpose or nature of material usage: standard production, rework/reprocessing, trial run, quality sample, or waste/scrap.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or username of the person who created this consumption record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lot consumption record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data governance and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the cost values are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  fifo_fefo_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the order in which lots were consumed according to FIFO or FEFO inventory management rules. Lower numbers indicate earlier consumption priority.']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'Classification of the consumed material regarding genetically modified organism content: GMO (contains GMO), non-GMO (verified non-GMO), or unknown (not tested/declared).']
  halal_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the consumed lot is certified halal according to Islamic dietary law. Required for halal product manufacturing and labeling.']
  kosher_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the consumed lot is certified kosher by a recognized kosher certification agency. Required for kosher product manufacturing and labeling.']
  lot_expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration or best-before date of the consumed lot. Used to enforce FEFO consumption rules and ensure materials are used before expiration.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'The unique document number generated by the ERP system for this goods movement transaction. Used for audit trail and transaction traceability.']
  material_document_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the material document was posted. Combined with material_document_number forms a unique key in the source system.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or username of the person who last modified this consumption record. Used for accountability and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lot consumption record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change for audit and data quality purposes.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'The SAP movement type code that classifies the inventory transaction (e.g., 261 for goods issue to production order, 262 for reversal). Determines how the consumption affects inventory and financial accounts.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the consumed lot is certified organic according to USDA NOP or equivalent organic certification standards. Critical for organic product integrity and labeling compliance.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the consumption transaction was posted to the financial and inventory ledgers. May differ from consumption_timestamp for backdated postings.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'The material reservation document number that allocated this lot for consumption against the production order. Links consumption to the planned material requirement.']
  supplier_lot_number varchar [note: 'The original lot or batch number assigned by the supplier for the consumed material. Critical for traceability back to the raw material source and supplier quality investigations.']
  total_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total cost of the consumed quantity, calculated as consumption_quantity * unit_cost. Represents the material cost component of the production order.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard or actual cost per unit of measure for the consumed material at the time of consumption. Used for production cost calculation and COGS determination.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the consumption quantity is measured (e.g., KG, LB, EA, L, GAL). Must align with the material master unit of measure.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The consumption variance expressed as a percentage of the BOM quantity. Calculated as (variance_quantity / bom_quantity) * 100.']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The difference between actual consumption quantity and the BOM planned quantity. Positive values indicate over-consumption; negative values indicate under-consumption.']
  warehouse_location_code varchar [note: 'The storage location or warehouse from which the material lot was withdrawn for consumption. Links to the warehouse management system location master.']

  Note: 'Transactional records of raw material, ingredient, and packaging component lot consumption against production orders, implementing FIFO/FEFO inventory management. Captures production order, batch record, consumed material code, consumed lot number, consumption quantity, unit of measure, consumption timestamp, FIFO/FEFO sequence number, expiry date of consumed lot, warehouse storage location, consumption type (standard, rework, trial), variance from BOM quantity, and confirmation status. Provides full lot traceability from raw material to finished goods batch. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM goods movement and MES batch management.'
}

Table manufacturing.routing {
  routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production routing master record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Standard cost estimates in F&B ERP are built from routings; each routing operation references a cost center to apply the correct overhead rate. This link is required for standard cost roll-up, activit']
  finance_standard_cost_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_standard_cost. Business justification: In F&B, standard cost estimates are directly derived from routings (machine/labor time) and BOMs. Linking routing to finance_standard_cost enables cost roll-up traceability, standard cost release audi']
  food_safety_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.food_safety_plan. Business justification: Production routings define the process steps where CCPs are applied. FSMA requires that process controls documented in the food safety plan are linked to the specific routing operations — this FK enab']
  market_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market. Business justification: F&B routings are market-specific due to differing regulatory requirements (EU allergen handling, USDA organic steps, Halal processing requirements by market). Market-specific routing variants are stan']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this routing is executed.']
  routing_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party co-packing supplier if this routing represents externally manufactured production. Null for internal production.']
  routing_toll_manufacturer_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the toll manufacturing supplier if this routing represents production where the company provides raw materials and the supplier provides processing. Null for internal or co-packed production.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finished good SKU that this routing produces.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary production line or work center group where this routing is executed. Used for capacity planning and scheduling.']
  allergen_handling_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing requires special allergen handling procedures, such as line changeover cleaning or dedicated equipment, to prevent cross-contact.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The current approval state of this routing. Approved routings are released for production use; pending routings await engineering or quality approval; rejected routings require rework; expired routings have passed their review date.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this routing was approved for production use.']
  base_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The standard production lot size for which the routing times are defined. Actual production order times are scaled proportionally from this base.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the base quantity (e.g., KG, LB, EA, CS). Aligns with the SKU primary UOM.']
  changeover_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'The standard time required to change over the production line from the previous SKU to this routing, including cleaning, setup, and first-article inspection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this routing record was first created in the system.']
  gmp_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing has been validated for GMP compliance. True means all operations meet GMP documentation, training, and control requirements.']
  haccp_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing contains one or more HACCP critical control points (CCPs) that require monitoring and documentation per the food safety plan.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date when this routing was last reviewed and validated by engineering or production management.']
  lot_size_from decimal(15,3) [note: 'The minimum production lot size for which this routing is valid. Supports lot-size-dependent routing selection.']
  lot_size_to decimal(15,3) [note: 'The maximum production lot size for which this routing is valid. Null indicates no upper limit.']
  maximum_batch_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'The largest production quantity that can be produced in a single batch using this routing, constrained by equipment capacity or process limits.']
  minimum_batch_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'The smallest production quantity that can be economically or technically produced using this routing, expressed in the base UOM.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this routing record was last modified.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next periodic review of this routing to ensure continued accuracy and compliance.']
  operation_count varchar [note: 'The total number of sequential operations (work center steps) defined in this routing.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing is certified for organic production under USDA NOP or equivalent standards, requiring segregation and documentation.']
  rework_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether non-conforming product from this routing can be reworked through a defined rework operation sequence.']
  routing_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the routing, typically summarizing the production method or key process characteristics (e.g., \'High-speed aseptic filling line\', \'Manual batch mixing and packaging\').']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the routing. The externally-known unique code used to reference this routing in production planning and scheduling systems.']
  routing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing. Active routings are available for production order creation; draft and under-review routings are in development; inactive and obsolete routings are no longer used.']
  routing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the routing. Standard routings are the default production method; alternative routings provide flexibility for capacity or material constraints; trial routings support NPD; co-packing and toll manufacturing routings reflect external production arrangements.']
  scrap_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The expected percentage of material loss or waste across all operations in this routing, used for material planning and costing.']
  total_standard_production_time_minutes decimal(12,2) [note: 'The sum of all operation standard times (setup, machine, labor) across the routing, expressed in minutes. Used for capacity planning and production scheduling.']
  usage varchar [note: 'The business purpose for which this routing is maintained. Production routings drive MES execution; costing routings feed standard cost calculations; maintenance and quality inspection routings support non-production work orders.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this routing version becomes effective for production planning and scheduling.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this routing version remains effective. Null indicates open-ended validity.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the routing. Allows multiple versions of the same routing to coexist for different time periods or production scenarios.']

  Note: 'Production routing master data defining the sequence of manufacturing operations (work center steps) required to produce a SKU. Captures routing number, routing version, SKU, plant, valid-from/to dates, routing status, total standard production time, and the ordered list of operations with their work center assignments, standard times (setup, machine, labor), and operation descriptions (e.g., mixing, pasteurization, filling, capping, labeling, palletizing). Sourced from SAP PP routing master. Distinct from the BOM (what goes in) — the routing defines how it is made and in what sequence.'
}

Table manufacturing.plant {
  plant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the manufacturing plant, co-packing facility, or production site. Primary key for the plant master data product.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: In ERP, every manufacturing plant is assigned to exactly one company code — a foundational organizational mapping required for statutory financial reporting, intercompany transactions, tax jurisdictio']
  establishment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: Each plant must be linked to its regulatory establishment registration for audit and reporting.']
  market_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market. Business justification: F&B plants are designated to serve specific geographic markets, driving regulatory compliance (FDA vs. EU vs. LATAM requirements), brand distribution planning, and market-specific production capacity ']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Logistics planning maps each plant to a distribution network node for transportation scheduling and OTIF performance reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: F&B plants are mapped to profit centers for segment and geographic P&L reporting. This link supports plant-level profitability analysis, transfer pricing between plants, and management reporting by ma']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the plant location including street number and name.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional location details such as building number, suite, or floor.']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the daily capacity metric (e.g., cases, pallets, kilograms, pounds, liters, gallons) aligned with the primary output of the facility.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the plant is located.']
  cold_storage_capacity_pallets varchar [note: 'Refrigerated and frozen storage capacity at the plant site measured in standard pallet positions for temperature-sensitive products and ingredients.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the plant was first commissioned and began commercial production operations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating the country where the plant is located (e.g., USA, MEX, CAN, DEU, CHN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this plant master data record was first created in the system.']
  daily_capacity_units decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum daily production capacity of the plant measured in standard production units per day. Used for capacity planning, demand allocation, and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculations.']
  decommissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the plant was permanently closed and ceased all production operations. Null for active facilities.']
  erp_plant_code varchar [note: 'Plant code or site identifier used in the primary ERP system (SAP S/4HANA, Oracle Cloud ERP) for transactional processing, inventory management, and financial reporting. May differ from the business plant_code for legacy integration reasons.']
  fda_registration_number varchar [note: 'FDA facility registration number required for food manufacturing facilities under FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) regulations. Mandatory for US-based facilities and importers.']
  gfsi_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the current GFSI certification requiring renewal to maintain compliance and customer approval status.']
  gfsi_scheme varchar [note: 'The GFSI-benchmarked food safety certification scheme held by the plant. SQF (Safe Quality Food), BRC (British Retail Consortium), FSSC 22000 (Food Safety System Certification), IFS (International Featured Standards), or none if not certified.']
  gmp_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the plant holds current GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) certification required for food and beverage manufacturing quality standards.']
  halal_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the plant holds halal certification from a recognized Islamic authority enabling production of halal-certified products.']
  kosher_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the plant holds kosher certification from a recognized rabbinical authority enabling production of kosher-certified products.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this plant master data record was most recently updated or modified.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the plant location in decimal degrees format for mapping and logistics optimization.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the plant location in decimal degrees format for mapping and logistics optimization.']
  mes_site_code varchar [note: 'Site or facility identifier used in the Manufacturing Execution System (Aveva/Wonderware MES) for production scheduling, batch management, and OEE tracking.']
  number_of_production_lines varchar [note: 'Total count of active production lines or manufacturing lines within the plant used for production scheduling and line allocation.']
  operating_days_per_week varchar [note: 'Standard number of operating days per week for the plant (typically 5, 6, or 7) used for production planning and capacity calculations.']
  operating_hours_per_day decimal(4,1) [note: 'Standard operating hours per day for the plant used for capacity planning and OEE calculations. Typically 8, 16, or 24 hours depending on shift structure.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the plant holds organic certification (USDA Organic, EU Organic, or equivalent) enabling production of certified organic products.']
  organic_certifying_body varchar [note: 'Name of the accredited organic certification body that issued the organic certification (e.g., QAI, CCOF, Oregon Tilth, Ecocert).']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the plant used across operational systems. Typically a 4-10 character alphanumeric code used in ERP systems (SAP plant code, Oracle site code).']
  plant_name varchar [note: 'Official business name of the manufacturing plant or production facility.']
  plant_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the plant in its lifecycle. Active indicates full production operations, idle indicates temporarily not producing but maintained, decommissioned indicates permanently closed, under construction indicates new facility being built, and temporarily closed indicates short-term shutdown for maintenance or seasonal operations.']
  plant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the facility type indicating ownership and operational model. Owned manufacturing indicates company-operated production facilities, co-packer indicates third-party contract manufacturing, toll manufacturer indicates facilities where the company provides ingredients and the manufacturer provides processing, pilot plant indicates small-scale testing facilities, R&D facility indicates research and development sites, and distribution center indicates warehousing and logistics hubs.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the plant address used for mail delivery and logistics routing.']
  primary_product_category varchar [note: 'Primary product category or product line manufactured at this plant (e.g., beverages, snacks, dairy, bakery, confectionery) used for portfolio planning and specialization tracking.']
  region varchar [note: 'Business-defined geographic region or market area for reporting and planning purposes (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC, Latin America).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or regional administrative division where the plant is located.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the plant location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London, Asia/Shanghai) used for production scheduling and shift management.']
  usda_establishment_number varchar [note: 'USDA establishment number assigned to facilities that process meat, poultry, or egg products under USDA inspection. Required for facilities producing USDA-regulated products.']
  warehouse_capacity_pallets varchar [note: 'Total warehouse storage capacity at the plant site measured in standard pallet positions for finished goods and raw material inventory management.']

  Note: 'Master data for all manufacturing plants, co-packing facilities, and production sites operated or contracted by the business. Captures plant code, plant name, plant type (owned manufacturing, co-packer, toll manufacturer, pilot plant, R&D facility), address, country, region, plant manager, plant capacity (units/day), number of production lines, GMP certification status, GFSI scheme (SQF, BRC, FSSC 22000), certification expiry date, FDA registration number, USDA establishment number, kosher certification flag, halal certification flag, organic certification flag, plant status (active, idle, decommissioned), and ERP plant code. SSOT for manufacturing facility master data.'
}

  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model designation for the equipment, used for parts ordering and technical documentation reference.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required calibration or verification activity to maintain equipment qualification status.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special handling instructions, or contextual information about the equipment that does not fit structured fields.']
  oee_baseline_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OEE percentage established as the performance baseline for this equipment, used for monitoring and continuous improvement initiatives. OEE measures availability, performance, and quality.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the equipment indicating its availability for production use.']
  preventive_maintenance_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Reference code linking this equipment to its preventive maintenance plan schedule defining frequency and tasks for routine maintenance activities.']
  rated_capacity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum production capacity of the equipment as specified by the manufacturer or validated during commissioning, expressed in the unit of measure appropriate to the equipment type.']
  safety_certification_code varchar [note: 'Reference code or identifier for safety certifications held by this equipment (e.g., UL, CE, CSA), documenting compliance with electrical and mechanical safety standards.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to this specific equipment unit for warranty and service tracking.']
  technical_specification_document_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the technical specification document containing detailed engineering specifications, operating parameters, and maintenance procedures for this equipment.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer\'s warranty coverage for this equipment expires, after which repairs and parts are no longer covered under warranty.']

  Note: 'Master data for all production equipment assets within manufacturing plants — filling machines, mixers, blenders, pasteurizers, extruders, packaging lines, labelers, metal detectors, checkweighers, and CIP systems. Captures equipment tag number, equipment name, equipment type, manufacturer, model, serial number, plant, work center assignment, installation date, commissioning date, rated capacity, current status (operational, under maintenance, decommissioned), preventive maintenance schedule reference, last calibration date, next calibration due date, calibration certificate number, OEE baseline target, and MES asset tag. SSOT for production equipment master data used in OEE, maintenance, and calibration tracking.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_line {
  production_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for production_line']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: OEE, downtime costs, and line-level manufacturing overhead are tracked per production line against cost centers for overhead absorption and variance reporting. F&B finance teams require this link for ']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Production lines (e.g., bottling lines, packaging lines) are capitalized as fixed assets in F&B. Linking production_line to fixed_asset supports capex tracking, depreciation allocation per line, asset']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility location where the line resides.']
  output_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Each production line has a designated finished goods staging storage location where completed products are placed before transfer to warehouse. Warehouse management and production scheduling use this ']
  parent_production_line_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on production_line (parent_production_line_id)']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant containing the line.']
  capacity_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum production capacity per hour (units per hour).']
  changeover_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Average time required to change over the line between products.']
  control_system varchar [note: 'Type of control system governing the line.']
  downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Accumulated downtime minutes for the reporting period.']
  energy_consumption_kwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total energy used by the line in kilowatt‑hours.']
  gmp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates if the line meets Good Manufacturing Practice requirements.']
  haccp_status varchar [note: 'Current HACCP compliance status for the line.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line is fully automated.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety inspection.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the line was last maintained.']
  last_quality_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent quality inspection.']
  line_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the line.']
  line_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the line\'s purpose and characteristics.']
  line_end_date date [note: 'Date the line was retired or removed from service.']
  line_group varchar [note: 'Logical group or family the line belongs to (e.g., primary, secondary).']
  line_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the production line.']
  line_operating_mode varchar [note: 'Mode in which the line operates.']
  line_speed_m_per_min decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical speed of the line in meters per minute.']
  line_start_date date [note: 'Date the production line was first put into service.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Functional type of the line (e.g., bottling, mixing).']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Status of the line\'s maintenance cycle.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next maintenance activity.']
  oee_actual decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured OEE percentage for the line.']
  oee_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned OEE percentage for the line.']
  production_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the line.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated quality score for the line (0‑100).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the production line record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the production line record.']
  safety_incident_count varchar [note: 'Number of safety incidents recorded for the line.']
  safety_status varchar [note: 'Current safety condition of the line.']
  setup_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Average time to set up the line for a production run.']
  uptime_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of time the line was operational.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for production_line. Referenced by production_line_id.'
}

Table quality.haccp_plan {
  haccp_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for haccp_plan']
  bill_of_materials_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bill_of_materials. Business justification: HACCP hazard analysis requires ingredient-level review via the BOM — each component is assessed for biological, chemical, and physical hazards. Codex Alimentarius HACCP Principle 1 mandates ingredient']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Regulatory safety reports are organized per brand; associating each HACCP plan with its brand supports brand‑level safety documentation and audit trails.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.center. Business justification: Distribution centers handling refrigerated, frozen, or allergen-containing products must operate under a facility-specific HACCP plan (FDA 21 CFR Part 117, FSMA PCHF rule). Linking haccp_plan directly']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: HACCP plan development, validation studies, and revalidation activities incur costs (consultant fees, lab studies, documentation) charged to cost centers. F&B food safety managers track HACCP program ']
  establishment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: HACCP plans are required per registered facility; linking enables compliance reporting and audit traceability.']
  food_safety_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.food_safety_plan. Business justification: HACCP plans implement the regulatory food safety plan (FSMA Preventive Controls). FDA requires HACCP plans to be traceable to the food safety plan on file at the establishment. A food safety expert wo']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: HACCP plans are formulation-dependent — processing parameters, CCPs, and hazard controls are defined per formulation version. Any formulation change triggers mandatory HACCP revalidation (FDA FSMA 21 ']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: HACCP plan scoping: HACCP plans are developed for specific processing facilities, including co-packers and contract manufacturing nodes in the supply network. Linking haccp_plan to network_node enable']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: FDA FSMA and Codex Alimentarius require HACCP plans to be scoped to specific products. F&B regulatory teams link HACCP plans to SKUs for product-specific hazard analysis, regulatory submissions, and a']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'Pre‑defined criteria that must be met for validation to be successful.']
  allergen_changeover_validation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether allergen changeover cleaning validation has been performed.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the HACCP plan.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the plan was approved.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the food‑safety professional who approved the plan.']
  cleaning_validation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used for cleaning validation (e.g., CIP, visual inspection).']
  confidence_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence (percentage) associated with validation results.']
  doc_control_number varchar [note: 'Control number used for document management and version control.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the HACCP plan expires or is superseded (nullable for open‑ended plans).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the HACCP plan becomes effective.']
  equipment_qualification_study varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment qualification (IQ/OQ/PQ) study linked to the plan.']
  execution_end_date date [note: 'Date when validation activities were completed.']
  execution_start_date date [note: 'Date when validation activities commenced.']
  haccp_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the HACCP plan.']
  hazard_categories varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of hazard categories addressed by the plan.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the validation activity.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier assigned to the HACCP plan (e.g., HACCP-2024-001).']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plan (e.g., product, facility, process).']
  protocol_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the validation protocol document.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the HACCP plan record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the HACCP plan record.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) that drive the HACCP plan (e.g., FDA FSMA Preventive Controls).']
  revalidation_interval_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days between required re‑validations.']
  revalidation_trigger varchar [note: 'Condition that initiates a re‑validation (e.g., product change, equipment upgrade).']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the plan\'s scope (products, processes, facilities covered).']
  test_results_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level summary of test outcomes and observations.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the HACCP plan record.']
  validation_scope varchar [note: 'Specific processes, equipment, or areas covered by the validation.']
  validation_type varchar [note: 'Category of validation associated with the plan.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the HACCP plan (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the HACCP plan record.']

  Note: 'Master record for Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) plans per production facility and product category, including all associated process validation, cleaning validation, and equipment qualification studies. Captures plan version, scope, hazard categories (biological, chemical, physical, radiological), regulatory basis (FDA FSMA Preventive Controls, USDA FSIS HACCP, Codex Alimentarius), approval status, effective dates, and responsible food safety team. Includes validation records: validation type (process/thermal, cleaning/CIP, equipment IQ/OQ/PQ, aseptic), scope, protocol reference, execution dates, acceptance criteria, test results summary, statistical confidence level, outcome (validated/failed), revalidation trigger conditions, and approval authority. Covers allergen changeover cleaning validation. SSOT for HACCP program governance, preventive control validation, process/cleaning/equipment qualification, and food safety plan management across all manufacturing sites.'
}

Table quality.critical_control_point {
  critical_control_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the critical control point record.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.center. Business justification: Distribution centers have defined CCPs for receiving dock temperature checks, cold storage monitoring, and cross-contamination prevention (FSMA Sanitary Transportation, GFSI standards). critical_contr']
  equipment_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of equipment used to enforce or monitor the CCP.']
  establishment_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.establishment_registration. Business justification: CCPs are defined for each registered plant; the FK supports regulatory mapping of critical limits to the establishment.']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: CCPs are defined based on formulation-specific processing parameters — pasteurization temperature for a dairy formulation differs from a juice formulation. Formulation changes require CCP revalidation']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent HACCP plan that includes this CCP.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line where the CCP is applied.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the most recent audit of this CCP.']
  audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the latest audit was performed.']
  ccp_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the CCP, often used in documentation and labeling.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the CCP with regulatory requirements.']
  control_point_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the control point measurement.']
  corrective_action_procedure varchar [note: 'Standard operating procedure to follow when a deviation occurs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the CCP record was created.']
  critical_control_point_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the CCP, e.g., "Cooking Temperature Check".']
  critical_control_point_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CCP.']
  critical_limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure associated with the critical limit value.']
  critical_limit_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold that must not be exceeded (or must be met) for the hazard.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system for the CCP definition (e.g., MES, manual entry).']
  deviation_trigger varchar [note: 'Condition that triggers a deviation (e.g., "temperature > 75°C").']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the CCP becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the CCP is retired or superseded (nullable).']
  escalation_path varchar [note: 'Defined escalation steps and contacts when a deviation occurs.']
  hazard_type varchar [note: 'Category of hazard the CCP is designed to control.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CCP is considered a critical control point (true) or a monitoring point (false).']
  last_measured_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric value recorded in the most recent observation.']
  last_measurement_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent observation (pass, fail, or out of range).']
  last_observed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent monitoring observation for this CCP.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of measurement captured at the CCP.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the measurement type.']
  monitoring_frequency_minutes varchar [note: 'How often the CCP must be monitored, expressed in minutes.']
  monitoring_method varchar [note: 'Method used to monitor the CCP (e.g., sensor, manual check, lab test).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any supplemental information about the CCP.']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Specific manufacturing step or operation where the CCP is applied.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to applicable regulatory requirement (e.g., FSMA, GFSI).']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with the CCP.']
  sampling_frequency_minutes varchar [note: 'How often sampling is performed, expressed in minutes.']
  sampling_method varchar [note: 'Method used to obtain the sample for measurement (e.g., random, systematic).']
  sampling_size varchar [note: 'Number of units or volume taken for each sample.']
  tolerance_lower decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value below the critical limit before a deviation is flagged.']
  tolerance_upper decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value above the critical limit before a deviation is flagged.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the CCP record.']
  verification_activity varchar [note: 'Periodic verification activity to ensure the CCP remains effective.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining each Critical Control Point (CCP) within a HACCP plan, plus all associated real-time monitoring observations recorded during production. Records the CCP number, process step, hazard type, critical limits (temperature, pH, Brix, water activity, time, metal detector sensitivity), monitoring method, monitoring frequency, corrective action procedure, verification activity, and responsible operator. Monitoring records capture each observation: batch/lot, timestamp, measured value, critical limit comparison, pass/fail result, operator ID, equipment/sensor ID, and deviation trigger with escalation path. Linked to the parent HACCP plan and specific production line. SSOT for CCP definition and all CCP monitoring data. Supports real-time food safety control and FSMA regulatory traceability.'
}

  Note: 'SAP QM-aligned entity representing quality inspection lots, their characteristic-level results, and statistical evaluations. Includes individual characteristic results: measured values (moisture, fat, pH, microbial count, organoleptic score), specification limits, valuation, inspection method, equipment used, and inspector. Supports statistical process control (SPC) with control charts, Cp/Cpk calculations, lot disposition decisions, and full quality inspection traceability across the supply chain. SSOT for all quality inspection events and measurements.'
}

  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final action taken for the affected product.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the non-conformance was first observed or recorded.']
  hold_duration_days varchar [note: 'Planned duration of the hold in days.']
  hold_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or storage location where the product is held.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the hold action.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Type of containment action applied to the affected product.']

  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the non-conformance record was created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the non-conformance record.']
  regulatory_report_type varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory reporting mechanism applicable to the event.']
  regulatory_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event must be reported to a regulatory authority.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Overall outcome status of the non-conformance handling process.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: process|material|equipment|human_error|supplier|design|environment|unknown — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_detail varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the identified root cause.']
  severity_classification varchar [note: 'Risk severity assigned to the non-conformance based on impact and likelihood.']
  severity_score varchar [note: 'Numeric severity rating (1‑5) where higher values indicate greater risk.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Origin of the non-conformance event, indicating how it was detected.']

  Note: 'Master record for all quality non-conformance events (NCRs), customer/consumer complaints, and product hold/containment actions. Captures NCR number, source (in-process, supplier, customer complaint, consumer hotline, audit finding, CCP deviation), affected SKU/batch/lot, defect type, complaint details (category, channel — call center, retailer, social media, DTC — description, investigation status, resolution), severity classification, regulatory reportability (FDA MedWatch, USDA, Reportable Food Registry), hold/containment details (hold type, warehouse location, segregation status, hold duration, disposition authority), disposition decision (release, rework, scrap, return to supplier, hold, destroy), root cause category, and originating inspection lot or CCP deviation. SSOT for all quality failures, deviations, complaints, containment holds, and disposition actions across the enterprise.'
}

  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: CAPA corrective‑action expenses are charged to a GL account to track corrective cost effectiveness.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Supplier CAPA traceability: CAPAs triggered by inbound material failures (out-of-spec delivery, contamination) must reference the specific inbound shipment for root cause investigation and supplier co']
  non_conformance_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the non‑conformance that triggered this CAPA, if applicable.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: CAPAs in F&B are product-specific — effectiveness verification, regulatory reporting (FDA 483 responses), and CAPA closure require the affected SKU to be formally identified. capa.product_code is a de']
  supplier_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_audit. Business justification: CAPAs in F&B are frequently raised from supplier audit findings (capa_required_flag on supplier_audit). Linking CAPA to the triggering supplier audit enables audit closure tracking, CAPA effectiveness']
  supplier_quality_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.supplier_quality_assessment. Business justification: CAPAs are triggered by supplier quality assessment findings (e.g., failed supplier audits, low quality scores). Adding supplier_quality_assessment_id to capa enables traceability from corrective actio']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_center. Business justification: CAPAs in F&B manufacturing are frequently tied to the work center where the root cause occurred (e.g., CIP failure, metal detector malfunction). \'All open CAPAs by work center\' is a standard quality o']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the CAPA actions were actually completed.']
  attached_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the CAPA record.']
  capa_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible CAPA reference number assigned by quality management (e.g., CAPA-2023-001).']
  capa_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CAPA.']
  capa_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record is a corrective action or a preventive action.']
  ccn_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the critical control point (CCP) related to the CAPA.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the CAPA record was formally closed.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or notes related to the CAPA.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action to be implemented.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAPA record was first created in the system.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for the CAPA.']
  effectiveness_verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the CAPA was effective.']
  effectiveness_verification_result varchar [note: 'Result of the effectiveness verification.']
  is_closed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CAPA record is closed (true) or still open (false).']
  is_effective boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CAPA was determined to be effective (true) or not (false).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent review of the CAPA effectiveness and status.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot number linked to the CAPA.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee owning the CAPA.']
  preventive_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the preventive action to avoid recurrence.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority for execution of the CAPA actions.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework or standard that the CAPA addresses (e.g., FDA, GFSI, ISO 22000).']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the risk level associated with the issue, used for prioritization.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the identified root cause.']
  root_cause_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to determine the root cause (e.g., 5-Why, Fishbone).']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level assigned to the issue addressed by the CAPA.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the CAPA actions should be completed.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title summarizing the corrective or preventive action.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the CAPA record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the effectiveness verification was performed.']
  verification_responsible varchar [note: 'Name of the person who performed the effectiveness verification.']

  Note: 'Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) master record linked to non-conformances, audit findings, customer complaints, or CCP deviations. Captures CAPA number, type (corrective/preventive), root cause analysis method (5-Why, Fishbone), root cause description, corrective actions defined, preventive actions defined, responsible owner, target completion date, actual completion date, effectiveness verification method, and closure status. Managed within Veeva Vault QMS.'
}

Table quality.micro_test_result {
  micro_test_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the microbiological test result record.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Microbiological test results must be traceable to the specific batch sampled — a core FDA/FSMA requirement for environmental monitoring and finished product testing. batch_number is denormalized on mi']
  cold_chain_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.cold_chain_event. Business justification: Temperature abuse during distribution accelerates microbial growth — when a cold chain excursion is detected, microbiological testing is mandated to assess product safety before disposition (FSMA, HAC']
  critical_control_point_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.critical_control_point. Business justification: Microbiological sampling in food manufacturing is frequently performed at Critical Control Points (e.g., pasteurization, cooking, chilling). Adding critical_control_point_id to micro_test_result links']
  equipment_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment used for the analysis.']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: Microbiological testing targets (APC, Listeria, Salmonella limits) are formulation-specific — water activity, pH, and preservative levels in the formulation determine micro risk thresholds. F&B lab te']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Microbiological testing in F&B is routinely performed on incoming raw materials at goods receipt (pathogen testing of produce, dairy, proteins). Linking micro results to goods receipt provides FSMA FS']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Inbound microbiological testing: raw materials and ingredients require micro testing (Salmonella, Listeria, TPC) upon receipt before release to production. micro_test_result.shipment_id references dis']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: In SAP QM-aligned food safety systems, microbiological test results are generated as part of an inspection lot. A micro test result belongs to a specific inspection lot, enabling traceability from lot']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_trace. Business justification: Microbial test results are performed on samples drawn from specific inventory lots. Lab managers and food safety teams need to link micro results to lot_trace for FSMA one-up-one-down traceability and']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.shipment. Business justification: Microbial test results for a batch are required for each shipment to meet regulatory compliance and quality release.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Microbial test results must be linked to SKU for the Microbial Testing Compliance Report required by FDA.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Micro test samples are drawn from specific stock positions. Lab managers need to link micro results to the sampled stock position to trigger quantity_quality_inspection updates and disposition decisio']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory is accredited for the test method.']
  analysis_date date [note: 'Date the laboratory completed the analysis.']
  analyst_name varchar [note: 'Name of the laboratory analyst who performed the test.']
  chain_of_custody varchar [note: 'Record of custody transfers for the sample from collection to analysis.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Date the sample was collected from the source.']
  collection_location varchar [note: 'Facility, line, or site where the sample was obtained.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the sample was taken, including time zone.']
  control_sample_type varchar [note: 'Type of control sample used (positive, negative, or blank).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test result record was first created in the system.']
  detection_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Analytical detection limit for the test method.']
  detection_limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the detection limit.']
  incubation_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the sample was incubated.']
  incubation_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Duration of incubation for the test.']
  is_control_sample boolean [note: 'True if the sample is a quality control (positive/negative) sample.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch number assigned to the material at production.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by the analyst.']
  pass_fail varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail determination based on result criteria.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the test result meets applicable regulatory criteria (e.g., FSMA limits).']
  result_interpretation varchar [note: 'Narrative interpretation of the test outcome by the analyst.']
  result_reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test result was officially reported to the requesting party.']
  result_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the result value.']
  result_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantitative test outcome (e.g., CFU count).']
  sample_condition varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the sample at collection.']
  sample_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the sample (e.g., supplier, in‑house production, field collection).']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Category of the sample (e.g., raw material, work‑in‑process, finished goods, environmental swab, water).']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Calculated shelf‑life in days based on the microbiological result.']
  target_organism varchar [note: 'Microbial organism or indicator targeted by the test.']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Standard analytical method used for the microbiological test.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the test.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the test result record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for microbiological testing including sample tracking and laboratory results. Covers sample registration (source, SKU/material, lot/batch, collection point, chain of custody, lab assignment, test panel requested) and test results (test method per AOAC/ISO/USDA FSIS, target organism — Salmonella, Listeria, E. coli O157:H7, TPC, Yeast & Mold — CFU count or presence/absence, detection limit, incubation conditions, lab accreditation, pass/fail). Applies to raw materials, WIP, finished goods, environmental swabs, and water samples. Critical for FSMA Preventive Controls and HACCP verification. SSOT for all microbiological sample-to-result tracking.'
}

Manages QMS controlled documents: document ID, type (SOP, work instruction, form, policy, specification, HACCP plan, validation protocol), title, version, effective/expiry dates, owner, approval workflow, training requirement flag, and applicable sites. SSOT for audit management, certification status, GFSI scheme compliance, and QMS document governance per ISO 9001 Clause 7.5.'
