  specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for specification']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand managers need direct access to product specifications for compliance checks and marketing claim substantiation; linking specification to brand enables brand‑level spec dashboards.']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: Quality specifications (pH, brix, moisture, fat%) are derived directly from formulation targets. When a formulation version changes, specifications must be updated and reapproved. F&B PLM/quality team']
  nutrition_label_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.nutrition_label. Business justification: Specification nutritional attributes (fat_percent, protein_percent, sodium_mg_per_kg, brix_percent) must reconcile with the approved nutrition label. Label-spec reconciliation is a mandatory pre-launc']
  packaging_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.packaging_spec. Business justification: Packaging quality specifications (seal integrity, barrier properties, headspace) are defined per packaging spec. F&B quality teams conduct incoming packaging inspection against these specs. Linking qu']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Product specifications must align with regulatory product registrations — allergen declarations, shelf life, and storage conditions on the spec must match the registered product dossier. Regulatory co']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Product specifications must reference SKU to ensure each SKU meets its defined quality specification.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person or system that approved the specification.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification was approved.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent compliance audit for this specification.']

  change_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative reason for the most recent revision.']
  color varchar [note: 'Standardized color designation for the product. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: red|green|blue|yellow|white|black|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Food safety or quality standard to which the specification adheres.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification record was first created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the specification becomes effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the specification expires, if applicable.']

  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the specification is considered critical for product safety or regulatory compliance.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent audit performed on the specification.']

  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable shelf life in days under specified storage conditions.']
  sodium_mg_per_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable sodium concentration in milligrams per kilogram.']
  spec_type varchar [note: 'Category of the specification (finished product, raw material, packaging, work‑in‑process).']
  specification_code varchar [note: 'Business-visible code uniquely identifying the specification within the organization.']
  specification_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the specification purpose and scope.']
  specification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the specification.']
  specification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the specification.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Recommended storage condition for the product.']
  tpc_cfu_g decimal(10,0) [note: 'Maximum total aerobic plate count per gram.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the specification record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the specification, following semantic versioning.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Target weight of the product or component in kilograms.']
  width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target width dimension in centimeters.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the specification record.']

  Note: 'Master record defining quality specifications for finished goods, raw materials, packaging, and WIP. Managed via TraceGains and SAP QM. Captures specification ID, material/SKU, specification type (finished product, raw material, packaging), version, effective date, physical parameters (weight, dimensions, color), chemical parameters (pH, Brix, moisture, fat, protein, sodium), microbiological limits (TPC, Salmonella, Listeria), organoleptic standards, and approval status. SSOT for quality acceptance criteria.'
}

Table quality.customer_complaint {
  customer_complaint_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for each complaint record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Required for Complaint Management Report: each complaint must be tied to the originating customer account for liability and service tracking.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Customer complaints in F&B are investigated at batch level — \'which batch caused this complaint?\' batch_number is denormalized on customer_complaint. A proper FK to batch_record enables structured com']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Complaints are analyzed per brand to drive brand reputation metrics and corrective actions; a brand FK provides the necessary aggregation.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the complainant (if known) linking to the customer master.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Customer complaint handling costs (replacement product, investigation labor, credit processing) are charged to cost centers for quality KPI and cost-of-poor-quality reporting. F&B quality teams track ']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: Customer complaints about taste, texture, or appearance trace back to specific formulation versions. F&B PLM and quality teams track complaint rates by formulation version to identify whether a formul']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.invoice. Business justification: Customer complaints in F&B frequently reference a specific invoice for financial deduction or credit memo processing. Complaint-to-invoice linkage is required for accounts receivable dispute resolutio']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_trace. Business justification: Customer complaints reference specific product lots for root cause investigation and potential recall triggering. Linking customer_complaint to lot_trace enables complaint-to-lot traceability required']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Customer Complaint Traceability: linking complaint to the original sales order enables root‑cause analysis, warranty handling, and service recovery.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: Promotion Impact & Complaint Correlation report links complaints to specific promotion events to assess promotion‑driven quality issues.']
  ship_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.ship_to_location. Business justification: Complaint investigation requires knowing the specific delivery location (retail store, DC) where defective product was received or consumed. Field quality teams use this to conduct on-site investigati']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Customer complaints are linked to SKU for the Complaint Resolution Process and regulatory reporting.']
  store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.store. Business justification: F&B complaint investigations require knowing the specific retail store where the product was purchased. Store-level complaint clustering drives field quality reviews, retailer scorecards, and root-cau']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the complaint was formally closed.']
  complainant_address varchar [note: 'Mailing address of the complainant, used for regulatory follow‑up if required.']
  complainant_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the complainant for follow‑up communication.']

  complaint_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the complaint, used for tracking and communication.']
  complaint_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the complaint was received or logged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the complaint record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial impact amount.']
  customer_complaint_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative provided by the complainant describing the issue.']
  customer_complaint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the complaint.']
  expected_resolution_date date [note: 'Target date for resolving the complaint as set during investigation.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary impact associated with the complaint (e.g., refunds, warranty costs).']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the internal investigation into the complaint.']
  product_upc varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code of the product linked to the complaint.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Regulatory body to which the complaint is reportable, if any.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complaint must be reported to a regulatory agency (e.g., FDA MedWatch, USDA).']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to resolve the complaint (e.g., product recall, reformulation, packaging change).']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified root cause of the issue after investigation.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level assigned to the complaint based on impact and risk.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the complaint was received.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the complaint record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for consumer and trade customer quality complaints received through all channels (call center, retailer, social media, DTC). Captures complaint ID, complaint source, product/SKU, batch/lot number, complaint category (foreign material, off-flavor, packaging defect, illness, labeling error), complaint description, severity, regulatory reportability flag (FDA MedWatch, USDA), investigation status, root cause, resolution action, and closure date. Supports FSMA reportable food registry obligations.'
}

Table quality.product_recall {
  product_recall_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each product recall event.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: FDA 21 CFR Part 7 recall regulations require direct notification to consignee customer accounts. Recall managers need to identify which accounts received affected lots to issue notifications, track ac']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: FDA/USDA recall regulations require tracing affected product to specific manufacturing batches. product_recall has affected_lot_codes as plain text but no FK to batch_record. A proper FK enables struc']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Recall investigations and communications are executed at the brand level; linking recalls to brand enables brand‑centric recall reporting and KPI tracking.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Recalls are executed per distribution center; linking recall to DC supports regulatory reporting and execution tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Product recalls incur direct costs (logistics, destruction, customer credits) charged to specific cost centers for recall cost management reporting. F&B finance teams require cost center assignment on']
  customer_complaint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.customer_complaint. Business justification: Product recalls can be initiated directly by consumer complaints (e.g., illness reports, foreign object complaints). Adding customer_complaint_id to product_recall enables traceability from the recall']
  formulation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: Product recalls in F&B are frequently caused by formulation issues — undeclared allergens, incorrect ingredient substitution, or formulation errors. FDA 21 CFR Part 7 recall reporting requires identif']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Recall financial loss is posted to a specific GL account for regulatory and management reporting.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Recall inbound traceability: a recall may require intercepting in-transit inbound shipments of affected raw materials or finished goods. Linking product_recall to inbound_shipment enables recall coord']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_trace. Business justification: Product recalls target specific inventory lots. Linking product_recall to lot_trace enables automated lot quarantine triggering and recall scope quantification. affected_lot_codes is a denormalized pl']
  non_conformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.non_conformance. Business justification: Product recalls are initiated based on non-conformance events (e.g., contamination, allergen mislabeling, foreign material). Adding non_conformance_id to product_recall provides the causal link from t']
  quarantine_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.quarantine_hold. Business justification: When a recall is initiated, inventory quarantine holds are placed on affected stock. Linking product_recall to quarantine_hold provides the recall-to-quarantine audit trail required for FDA/USDA recal']
  recall_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.recall_event. Business justification: Internal quality recall records must be linked to the official regulatory recall event filed with FDA/USDA. Recall closure and effectiveness reporting requires tracing the internal quality action to t']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Recall records need a direct SKU link for the Recall Management Process to identify affected products.']
  spec_quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Product recall events are associated with a specific product specification.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Product recalls in F&B frequently originate from supplier-sourced ingredient defects. Linking recall to the responsible supplier enables FDA/USDA regulatory reporting (21 CFR Part 7), supplier liabili']
  affected_sku_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of SKU identifiers impacted by the recall.']
  audit_report_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal audit report linked to the recall.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the recall with regulatory requirements.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned actions to prevent recurrence of the issue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recall record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for monetary loss amount.']
  distribution_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the recall distribution.']
  fda_notification_date date [note: 'Date the FDA was notified of the recall.']
  initiating_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the party (e.g., manufacturer, regulator) that triggered the recall.']
  is_voluntary boolean [note: 'True if the recall was initiated voluntarily by the company.']
  monetary_loss_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial loss associated with the recall, excluding recovered inventory.']
  press_release_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a public press release was issued for the recall.']
  product_recovery_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units successfully recovered from the market.']
  quantity_affected varchar [note: 'Total number of units (e.g., cases, bottles) affected by the recall.']
  recall_classification varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the recall purpose.']
  recall_closure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recall was officially closed.']
  recall_initiation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recall was formally initiated.']
  recall_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the recall by the company or regulator.']
  recall_reason varchar [note: 'Primary cause prompting the recall.']
  recall_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recall event.']
  recall_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the recall according to FDA definitions or voluntary withdrawal.']
  retailer_notification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of retailer notifications regarding the recall.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level of the recall based on severity and exposure.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the root cause investigation findings.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the recall record.']
  usda_notification_date date [note: 'Date the USDA was notified of the recall (if applicable).']

  Note: 'Master record for product recall and withdrawal events. Captures recall ID, recall type (Class I, II, III per FDA / voluntary withdrawal), triggering event (micro failure, allergen mislabel, foreign material, regulatory action), affected SKUs, affected lot/date codes, quantity affected, distribution scope, recall initiation date, FDA/USDA notification date, press release issued flag, retailer notification status, product recovery quantity, and recall closure date. SSOT for recall management and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table quality.supplier_quality_assessment {
  supplier_quality_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each supplier quality assessment record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Supplier assessment costs are allocated to a cost center for budgeting and performance measurement.']
  food_safety_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.food_safety_audit. Business justification: Supplier quality assessments are often conducted in conjunction with or reference food safety audits of the supplier facility. Adding food_safety_audit_id normalizes the relationship. The existing aud']
  gfsi_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.gfsi_certification. Business justification: Supplier quality assessments evaluate and reference the supplier\'s GFSI certification (BRC, SQF, FSSC 22000) to verify validity and scope. Approved supplier list management and supplier risk tiering d']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Supplier quality assessments incur audit fees, third-party certification costs, and travel expenses posted to GL accounts. F&B procurement quality teams post assessment costs to specific GL accounts f']
  spec_quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Supplier quality assessments are performed against product specifications.']
  supplier_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_audit. Business justification: In F&B, the quality team\'s supplier quality assessment is directly linked to the procurement supplier audit event. Linking these enables unified audit-to-assessment traceability, CAPA closure tracking']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier being assessed.']
  supplier_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_site. Business justification: GFSI/SQF/BRC assessments in F&B are conducted at a specific supplier manufacturing site, not just the supplier header. Site-level assessment drives approved vendor list status per site and regulatory ']
  approved_supplier_list_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the supplier on the company\'s Approved Supplier List.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date the assessment was performed or officially recorded.']
  assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments and observations recorded during the assessment.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the assessment for tracking and communication with suppliers.']
  assessment_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the assessment indicating overall compliance.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Method used to conduct the supplier quality assessment.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the quality professional who performed the assessment.']
  brc_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds a valid BRC certification.']
  brix_value decimal(4,2) [note: 'Sugar content measurement of the sample expressed in degrees Brix.']
  cap_action_implemented boolean [note: 'Indicates whether identified corrective and preventive actions have been completed.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current certification status of the supplier under relevant schemes.']
  cfu_count varchar [note: 'Microbiological count (CFU) observed in sampled material.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier meets all required compliance criteria.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline for implementing any required corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment identified issues that need corrective action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment record was first created in the system.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the assessment data originated.']
  document_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the quality management document linked to this assessment.']
  food_safety_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) reflecting the supplier\'s food‑safety performance.']
  fssc_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds a valid FSSC 22000 certification.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the quality manager who performed the final review of the assessment.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the final review was completed.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot identifier associated with the sampled material.']
  material_category varchar [note: 'Category of the material or ingredient supplied (e.g., raw ingredient, packaging, finished good).']
  non_conformance_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct non‑conformances identified during the assessment.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted aggregate of food safety, quality, and compliance scores.']
  ph_value decimal(4,2) [note: 'Measured acidity/alkalinity of the sample.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) reflecting overall product quality performance of the supplier.']
  reassessment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the supplier must be re‑assessed to maintain approval.']
  regulated_product_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessed material is subject to specific regulatory controls.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) governing the assessment criteria.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the supplier based on assessment outcomes.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of samples taken for laboratory testing.']
  sampling_method varchar [note: 'Method used to select samples for testing during the assessment.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Estimated shelf life of the material in days based on assessment.']
  sqf_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds a valid SQF certification.']
  supplier_quality_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assessment.']
  supplier_rating varchar [note: 'Overall rating assigned to the supplier based on assessment results.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the assessment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for supplier quality evaluations and approved supplier assessments conducted by the quality team. Captures assessment ID, supplier, material/ingredient category, assessment type (questionnaire, on-site audit, desk review, third-party certificate review), assessment date, SQF/BRC/FSSC 22000 certification status, food safety score, quality score, approved supplier list (ASL) status, risk tier, and re-assessment due date. Supports TraceGains supplier compliance management.'
}

Table quality.hold_record {
  hold_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the hold record.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Quality holds in F&B are placed on specific batches. hold_record has batch_number as a denormalized plain attribute but no FK to batch_record. Linking enables \'which batch is under hold?\' — critical f']
  bill_of_materials_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bill_of_materials. Business justification: Inventory holds in F&B are often triggered by BOM-level issues — a suspect ingredient lot affects all batches produced with that BOM version. Linking hold_record to bill_of_materials enables quality t']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Inventory holds are managed at specific distribution centers; linking hold_record to the DC enables inventory visibility and recall coordination.']
  cold_chain_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.cold_chain_event. Business justification: Cold chain excursion events are the most common trigger for quality holds on food products in distribution. Linking hold_record directly to the initiating cold_chain_event supports FSMA disposition de']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Holding inventory incurs cost; linking to a cost center enables accurate inventory holding cost accounting.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Inventory holds result in write-offs, destruction costs, or rework charges posted to GL accounts in F&B ERP. Financial posting of hold-related losses requires direct GL account reference on hold recor']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Holds placed on incoming materials at goods receipt require reference to the receipt event for supplier notification, return-to-vendor processing, and regulatory documentation. F&B quality operations ']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: A hold is often placed on a specific inspection lot that failed quality inspection. Adding inspection_lot_id to hold_record links the hold directly to the inspection lot, enabling traceability from lo']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_trace. Business justification: Quality holds are placed on specific inventory lots. Linking hold_record to lot_trace enables FSMA one-up-one-down traceability and lot-level hold disposition reporting. hold_record has lot_number as ']
  non_conformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.non_conformance. Business justification: Product holds are frequently placed as a direct result of a non-conformance event. Adding non_conformance_id to hold_record provides the causal link between the quality event and the inventory action,']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Order Hold Management: holds are often placed on specific sales orders pending quality investigation; linking provides order status and financial impact visibility.']
  product_recall_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.product_recall. Business justification: During a product recall event, holds are placed on affected inventory. Adding product_recall_id to hold_record links the hold to the recall that triggered it, enabling complete recall scope tracking a']
  quarantine_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.quarantine_hold. Business justification: Quality hold_record (quality management layer) and inventory quarantine_hold (WMS/inventory layer) represent the same physical hold at different system layers. QA managers reconciling quality holds ag']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.shipment. Business justification: Quality holds are routinely placed on in-transit or staged shipments (e.g., temperature excursion detected mid-route, regulatory stop-ship). A direct FK from hold_record to shipment enables the distri']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Quality hold records must reference SKU to manage inventory holds per specific product SKU.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Hold management process needs to tie a hold record to the exact stock position being quarantined for traceability and release decisions.']
  attached_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents (e.g., lab reports, audit findings) attached to the hold record.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory compliance status of the held material.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final action taken on the held material (release, rework, destroy, return to supplier, or quarantine).']
  disposition_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the chosen disposition action.']
  disposition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the disposition decision was executed.']
  hold_creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hold record was first created in the system.']
  hold_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the hold is scheduled to be automatically released if no disposition occurs.']
  hold_initiated_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who created the hold record.']
  hold_initiated_department varchar [note: 'Business department that originated the hold request.']
  hold_initiation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the hold was placed.']
  hold_last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the hold record.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the hold transaction.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description explaining why the hold was initiated.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the hold (e.g., active, released, rework, destroyed, pending, closed).']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the hold reason (quality, microbiological, allergen, regulatory, or customer complaint).']
  is_fefo_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold respects First‑Expired‑First‑Out inventory rules.']
  is_fifo_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold respects First‑In‑First‑Out inventory rules.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for the batch of material placed on hold.']
  material_upc varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code of the material, 12‑digit numeric identifier.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text comments or observations related to the hold.']
  quality_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the quality manager who authorized the hold.']
  quantity_held decimal(15,3) [note: 'Amount of material placed on hold, expressed in the selected unit of measure.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number from FDA, USDA, or other regulator linked to the hold.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the held quantity (e.g., kilogram, gram, pound, unit, case, liter).']
  warehouse_location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or storage location where the hold is physically applied.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for product holds (quality, micro, allergen, regulatory, customer complaint) placed on finished goods, WIP, or raw materials pending quality disposition. Captures hold type, affected material/SKU, lot/batch, quantity, hold initiation date, reason, warehouse location, disposition decision (release, rework, destroy, return to supplier), disposition date, and authorizing quality manager. Integrates with warehouse management for physical segregation and supports FIFO/FEFO inventory management, lot traceability, and regulatory containment requirements.'
}

Table quality.haccp_plan_specification {
  haccp_plan_specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the HACCP plan specification linkage record']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to haccp_plan']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to specification']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of how this specification applies to the HACCP plan (e.g., critical limit, monitoring parameter, verification requirement). Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  critical_limit_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific critical limit parameter in the specification (e.g., pH, temperature, microbial count) that this HACCP plan monitors. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this specification became applicable to this HACCP plan. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this linkage expires or is superseded (nullable for ongoing linkages)']
  hazard_category_mapping varchar [note: 'Mapping of which hazard categories (biological, chemical, physical, radiological) from the HACCP plan are controlled by this specification. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  linkage_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this HACCP plan-specification linkage (active, superseded, under review, deprecated)']
  linked_by varchar [note: 'Name or ID of the food safety professional who established this linkage']
  linked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this HACCP plan-specification linkage was created']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes about this specific HACCP plan-specification linkage']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory requirement (FDA FSMA, USDA FSIS, Codex) that mandates this linkage']
  version_at_plan_approval varchar [note: 'Version number of the specification that was in effect when the HACCP plan was approved. Critical for regulatory audit trail. Identified in detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the regulatory linkage between HACCP plans and quality specifications. It captures the formal relationship between hazard control plans and the critical limit specifications they reference. Each record links one HACCP plan to one specification with effective dates, version tracking, and hazard category mapping that exist only in the context of this regulatory compliance relationship.. Existence Justification: In food manufacturing operations, HACCP plans reference multiple quality specifications to define critical limits for different hazard control points (e.g., temperature specs for thermal processing, pH specs for acidified foods, microbial limits for finished products), and each specification is referenced by multiple HACCP plans across different facilities, product lines, and process stages. The FDA FSMA Preventive Controls rule and USDA HACCP regulations require documented, version-controlled linkage between HACCP hazard analyses and the specifications that define critical limits, making this a regulatory compliance requirement that food safety teams actively manage.'
}

Table supply.demand_plan {
  demand_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each demand plan record at the SKU/DC/time-period granularity. Primary key for the demand_plan data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or channel account for which the demand plan is generated, when the plan is customer-specific (e.g., key account demand plan). Nullable for aggregate/market-level plans.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: F&B demand plans are structured at brand level for brand-level S&OP review (e.g., Lay\'s brand demand plan). Brand-level consensus_forecast_qty and promotional_uplift_qty are standard brand P&L inputs.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: S&OP financial reconciliation: consensus demand volume is compared against the revenue budget to identify gaps. F&B IBP processes explicitly reconcile demand plans to financial budgets; finance needs ']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: In F&B S&OP, demand planners link demand plans to specific marketing campaigns to quantify promotional_uplift_qty. Campaign-driven demand uplift is a named input in consensus demand review. No existin']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: F&B demand planning operates at category level for S&OP — Nielsen/IRI syndicated data (iri_category_code, nielsen_category_code on hierarchy) and top-down statistical forecasting aggregate at category']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Distribution Center (DC) or warehouse location for which the demand plan is generated. Supports network-level demand disaggregation across the DC network and 3PL partners.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Demand plans are bucketed by fiscal period for financial reporting alignment. F&B companies align demand planning horizons to fiscal periods for P&L forecasting and period-close demand reporting. plan']
  forecast_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.forecast. Business justification: S&OP financial reconciliation: demand plan consensus volume is reconciled against the financial forecast each planning cycle. F&B IBP practitioners explicitly link demand plans to the finance forecast']
  market_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market. Business justification: Global F&B demand plans are market-scoped (e.g., UK market Q3 plan). Market drives seasonality_index, syndicated_market_qty, and baseline_forecast_qty inputs. A domain expert expects demand_plan to re']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Demand plans are created at SKU/DC/time-period granularity per the product description. demand_plan already has center_id (cross-domain to distribution.center) for the distribution center dimension. A']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Demand planning incorporates product registration status to avoid forecasting for items pending regulatory approval.']
  promotion_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: Demand Planning incorporates promotion plans to adjust forecasted demand; linking enables the Demand Impact Forecast report.']
  purchase_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_contract. Business justification: S&OP demand planning in F&B must account for contract-committed supply volumes and price validity windows. Planners reference active purchase contracts to constrain demand plans to available contracte']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: F&B demand planners build differentiated forecasts by customer segment (grocery, foodservice, club, DTC). Linking segment_id to demand_plan enables segment-level forecast accuracy KPIs, S&OP demand re']
  ship_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.ship_to_location. Business justification: In F&B VMI and CPFR programs, demand plans are built at the customer ship-to location level (e.g., retailer DC or store). This FK enables location-level demand sensing, replenishment planning, and cus']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) for which the demand plan is generated. The SKU is the lowest-level product identifier used in demand planning and inventory management.']
  sop_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) / Integrated Business Planning (IBP) cycle in which this demand plan record was generated or last updated. Enables traceability of demand plan versions to specific planning cycles.']
  syndicated_market_data_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.syndicated_market_data. Business justification: F&B demand planners use Nielsen/IRI syndicated data as a primary statistical forecast input. demand_plan.syndicated_market_qty is a denormalized quantity from a specific syndicated_market_data record.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Territory-level demand disaggregation is a named F&B S&OP process — demand plans are segmented by sales territory to align regional forecasts with sales targets. A domain expert expects demand_plan to']
  adjusted_forecast_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Demand forecast quantity after planner-applied manual adjustments, overrides, or market intelligence inputs on top of the statistical baseline. Reflects demand planner judgment before consensus alignment.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the demand plan record was formally approved during the S&OP consensus cycle. Supports audit trail, SLA compliance tracking, and planning cycle performance measurement.']
  baseline_forecast_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Underlying demand forecast quantity representing the expected sell-through volume excluding any promotional uplift, new product launch effects, or extraordinary events. Used as the reference point for uplift/downlift factor calculation.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Trade channel for which this demand plan is generated (e.g., retail, foodservice, direct-to-consumer, e-commerce). Supports channel-level demand disaggregation and channel-specific supply planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail|foodservice|direct_to_consumer|ecommerce|club|convenience|drug|mass — promote to reference product]']
  consensus_forecast_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final agreed demand volume (in selling units) resulting from the cross-functional S&OP consensus process, incorporating inputs from Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Supply Chain. This is the primary input to supply planning and inventory optimization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this demand plan record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  customer_order_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Confirmed customer purchase order quantity for the SKU/DC/period, sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD or EDI order feeds. Represents committed demand and is used to override or supplement statistical forecasts in the near-term horizon.']
  demand_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the demand type for supply planning purposes. \'regular\' = baseline replenishment demand; \'promotional\' = trade promotion-driven demand; \'new_product\' = NPD launch demand; \'phase_out\' = end-of-life demand; \'safety_stock\' = buffer stock demand.']
  demand_sensing_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Near-term demand signal quantity derived from high-frequency data inputs including Point of Sale (POS) data, customer orders, and syndicated data (Nielsen/IRI). Used for daily/weekly supply execution and short-horizon replenishment decisions.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month or quarter number) within the fiscal year to which this demand plan record belongs. Supports period-over-period demand trend analysis and S&OP cycle alignment.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this demand plan record belongs, as defined by the company\'s financial calendar. Used for annual budgeting, S&OP alignment, and year-over-year demand comparison.']
  forecast_algorithm varchar [note: 'Statistical or machine learning algorithm used to generate the baseline statistical forecast for this SKU/period. Used for model governance, accuracy benchmarking, and algorithm selection optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exponential_smoothing|arima|moving_average|machine_learning|croston|holt_winters|causal — promote to reference product]']
  forecast_confidence_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level (expressed as a percentage, 0–100) associated with the demand forecast, indicating the reliability of the forecast model output. Used to flag low-confidence forecasts for planner review and safety stock buffer sizing.']
  forecast_error_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Historical Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of the forecasting model for this SKU/DC combination, expressed as a percentage. Used to assess forecast model accuracy, trigger model recalibration, and size safety stock buffers.']
  forecast_lower_bound_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower confidence interval bound for the demand forecast quantity, representing the low-end demand scenario. Used for inventory optimization, downside risk assessment, and supply-demand balancing.']
  forecast_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure in which all forecast quantity fields are expressed (e.g., CS = cases, EA = each, LB = pounds, KG = kilograms). Aligns with the selling unit of measure defined in the SAP S/4HANA material master. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|LB|KG|OZ|GAL|L|MT — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  forecast_upper_bound_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper confidence interval bound for the demand forecast quantity, representing the high-end demand scenario. Used for safety stock calculation, capacity planning, and supply risk management.']
  is_frozen boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the demand plan record is frozen (locked) for supply execution and cannot be modified by planners. Frozen plans are used as firm inputs to production scheduling and procurement within the planning fence horizon.']
  is_new_product boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this demand plan record is for a New Product Development (NPD) / new SKU launch, where no historical sales data exists for statistical forecasting. Triggers use of analogous product forecasting or market-based estimation methods.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the demand plan record, formatted as DP-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN. Used for cross-system traceability between SAP IBP, JDA/Blue Yonder, and ERP systems.']
  plan_period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the planning time bucket to which this demand plan record applies. Together with plan_period_start_date, defines the demand planning horizon window.']
  plan_period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the planning time bucket (day, week, or month) to which this demand plan record applies. Used to align demand signals with supply planning and inventory replenishment cycles.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demand plan record within the S&OP consensus cycle. \'statistical\' = system-generated baseline; \'adjusted\' = planner-modified; \'consensus\' = cross-functional agreed; \'approved\' = executive-signed-off; \'frozen\' = locked for supply execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|statistical|adjusted|consensus|approved|frozen|superseded — promote to reference product]']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the demand plan by its generation method and purpose. \'statistical\' = algorithm-generated baseline; \'consensus\' = S&OP agreed volume; \'customer_order\' = customer-committed orders; \'demand_sensing\' = near-term POS/syndicated signal; \'promotional\' = uplift-adjusted plan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: statistical|consensus|customer_order|demand_sensing|promotional — promote to reference product]']
  planner_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text commentary entered by the demand planner to document the rationale for manual forecast adjustments, market intelligence inputs, or exceptions. Supports audit trail and knowledge transfer within the S&OP process.']
  planning_horizon varchar [note: 'Time granularity of the demand plan bucket. \'daily\' and \'weekly\' are used for near-term demand sensing and supply execution; \'monthly\' is used for S&OP and IBP cycles; \'quarterly\' for long-range planning.']
  pos_sales_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual Point of Sale (POS) sell-through quantity for the SKU during the plan period, sourced from retailer POS data feeds. Used as a demand sensing input signal to update near-term forecasts and detect demand shifts.']
  promotional_uplift_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Incremental demand volume (in selling units) attributed to planned trade promotions, marketing campaigns, or temporary price reductions above the baseline forecast. Sourced from Trade Promotion Management (TPM) systems and applied as an uplift factor.']
  seasonality_index decimal(7,4) [note: 'Seasonal index factor for the SKU/period combination, representing the ratio of period demand to average annual demand. Values >1.0 indicate above-average seasonal demand; <1.0 indicate below-average. Used in statistical forecasting model decomposition.']
  sensing_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days into the future covered by the demand sensing signal for this plan record. Typically 1–14 days for near-term POS-driven demand sensing. Distinguishes short-horizon sensing from longer-horizon statistical forecasts.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system from which this demand plan record was generated or ingested. Used for data lineage tracking and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  statistical_forecast_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'System-generated baseline demand forecast quantity (in selling units) produced by the statistical forecasting algorithm (e.g., exponential smoothing, ARIMA, machine learning) in SAP IBP or JDA/Blue Yonder, prior to any manual adjustment or consensus override.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this demand plan record was last modified, including planner adjustments, consensus overrides, or system recalculations. Used for change tracking and incremental Silver Layer processing.']
  uplift_factor decimal(7,4) [note: 'Multiplicative factor applied to the baseline forecast to account for demand uplift (>1.0) or downlift (<1.0) from events such as trade promotions, new product launches, distribution gains/losses, or market disruptions. A value of 1.0 indicates no adjustment.']
  uplift_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason code explaining the cause of the uplift or downlift factor applied to the baseline forecast. Used for demand decomposition analysis and post-event forecast accuracy review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promotion|new_product_launch|distribution_gain|distribution_loss|seasonality|event|competitor_action|price_change|none — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Statistical and consensus-based demand forecast at SKU/DC/time-period granularity, serving as the primary input to supply planning and inventory optimization. Captures baseline statistical forecast, adjusted forecast, consensus volume, demand sensing signals (POS data, syndicated data, customer orders), uplift/downlift factors, confidence intervals, and sensing horizon. Supports both monthly S&OP planning horizons and daily/weekly demand signal refresh for near-term supply execution. Generated through demand sensing and S&OP consensus cycles.'
}

Table supply.plan {
  plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the supply plan record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  bill_of_materials_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bill_of_materials. Business justification: MRP and IBP supply plans execute against a specific BOM version — planned_production_qty, costing_lot_size, and yield calculations depend on the active BOM. Supply planners must know which BOM revisio']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand‑level supply planning aligns production, inventory, and distribution with the brand’s marketing objectives and sales targets.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Supply plans are compared against COGS and inventory budgets; finance runs plan-vs-budget variance reports. F&B supply finance teams validate planned production quantities against the approved budget ']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Supply plans in F&B are created for campaign-driven production runs (e.g., planned_production_qty uplift for a Super Bowl campaign). Linking plan to campaign enables campaign-to-supply traceability in']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Distribution Center (DC) or 3PL node in the supply network for which this plan defines planned receipts and shipments.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Supply planning must honor contracted volumes; linking supply_plan to sales_contract enables the Contract‑Based Production Planning process.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation of a production plan to an internal cost center for monthly financial reporting and variance analysis.']
  demand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.demand_plan. Business justification: A constrained supply plan is built on top of a consensus demand plan. The plan.demand_qty reflects the demand signal consumed from demand_plan. Adding demand_plan_id creates the explicit lineage from ']
  finance_standard_cost_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_standard_cost. Business justification: Supply planning uses standard costs to value planned production quantities and calculate planned COGS. F&B supply planners reference standard cost records when evaluating make-vs-buy decisions and pro']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Supply plans are versioned by fiscal period; finance uses supply plan data for COGS and inventory valuation in period-close. F&B supply planners always tie supply plans to fiscal periods for financial']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Supply plans are created at SKU/location granularity where location is a network node (DC or manufacturing plant). plan already has plant_id (cross-domain to manufacturing.plant) and center_id (cross-']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or production facility associated with this supply plan record. Used for SKU/plant-level planning granularity.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Supply plans must respect product registration status — production cannot be planned for de-registered or non-compliant SKUs. S&OP and MRP processes in F&B require validating registration status befor']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis of each supply plan by profit center to support P&L statements.']
  purchase_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_contract. Business justification: Master supply plans (MRP/IBP) are constrained by purchase contract terms (MOQ, volume commitments, lead times). Linking plan to purchase_contract enables contract-constrained supply planning and contr']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) for which this supply plan is generated. Drives replenishment signals and production scheduling at the SKU level.']
  sop_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.sop_cycle. Business justification: A constrained supply plan is produced as a direct output of an S&OP/IBP cycle. The plan.planning_cycle STRING attribute is a label, not a structured FK. Adding sop_cycle_id formalizes the governance r']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Supply allocation plans in F&B IBP/S&OP are disaggregated by sales territory to align supply commitments with regional sales targets. Territory-based supply planning is a standard named process in F&B']
  allocation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supply allocation decisions are required for this SKU/DC due to constrained supply. When true, the supply plan drives allocation logic in the order management and trade management systems.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the supply plan was formally approved by the designated S&OP approver. Null if the plan has not yet been approved. Part of IBP governance audit trail.']
  closing_inventory_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Projected on-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the end of the planning horizon after accounting for planned production, receipts, and shipments. Indicates supply-demand balance position.']
  co_packing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the planned production quantity for this SKU involves co-packing or toll manufacturing arrangements with a third-party contract manufacturer, affecting supply lead times and MOQ constraints.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supply plan record was first captured in the data platform. Used for audit trail and IBP governance.']
  demand_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total planned demand quantity for the SKU over the planning horizon as consumed from the demand plan. Represents the demand signal used for supply-demand balancing in IBP.']
  demand_sensing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the demand quantity in this supply plan was derived from SAP IBP Demand Sensing (short-horizon statistical signal) rather than the standard statistical forecast. Affects plan accuracy benchmarking.']
  horizon_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan. Defines the end boundary for planned production, receipts, and shipment quantities.']
  horizon_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan. Defines the start boundary for planned production, receipts, and shipment quantities.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Total supply lead time in calendar days for the SKU from production order release to DC receipt. Used in replenishment signal timing and safety stock calculations.']
  lot_size decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard production lot size for the SKU at the plant, used to round planned production quantities to feasible manufacturing batch sizes in the constrained supply plan.']
  moq decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) constraint applied to this SKU in the supply plan. Ensures production and procurement orders meet supplier or manufacturing minimum thresholds.']
  network_tier varchar [note: 'Identifies the tier of the supply network node (plant, primary DC, secondary DC, 3PL, cross-dock) for which this supply plan record is generated. Supports network design analytics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or commentary entered by the planner or approver describing key assumptions, constraints, or exceptions relevant to this supply plan version. Supports IBP governance documentation.']
  opening_inventory_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'On-hand inventory quantity for the SKU at the start of the planning horizon. Used as the baseline for supply-demand balance position calculations.']
  plan_date timestamp [note: 'The real-world business event timestamp when this supply plan version was generated or published in the IBP system. Distinct from the audit record creation timestamp.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the supply plan, used in IBP, ERP, and cross-functional S&OP communications. Follows the SP-{alphanumeric} naming convention.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the supply plan within the IBP approval workflow. Drives downstream replenishment signal release and production scheduling actions.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supply plan indicating whether it reflects capacity-constrained production, unconstrained demand, consensus S&OP output, baseline reference, or simulation scenario.']
  planned_production_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total planned production quantity for the SKU at the plant over the planning horizon, expressed in the SKU\'s base unit of measure. Key volume metric driving MES scheduling and capacity allocation.']
  planned_receipts_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total planned inbound receipts quantity for the SKU at the DC over the planning horizon, including transfers from plants and external supplier deliveries. Key volume metric for supply-demand balancing.']
  planned_shipments_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total planned outbound shipments quantity for the SKU from the DC over the planning horizon. Drives replenishment signals and allocation decisions when supply is constrained.']
  planning_cycle varchar [note: 'Identifies the IBP/S&OP planning cycle (e.g., monthly-2024-06) to which this supply plan belongs. Enables cycle-over-cycle comparison and governance tracking.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text reason provided by the approver when a supply plan is rejected in the IBP approval workflow. Null when plan_status is not \'rejected\'. Supports governance audit trail.']
  replenishment_signal_released boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the replenishment signal derived from this supply plan has been released to the ERP or WMS for execution. Tracks plan-to-execution handoff status.']
  rotation_policy varchar [note: 'Inventory rotation policy applied to this SKU in the supply plan. FIFO (First In First Out) and FEFO (First Expired First Out) are standard in food and beverage to minimize waste and ensure food safety compliance.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum buffer inventory quantity for the SKU at the DC/plant, set to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Drives replenishment trigger signals in IBP.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Remaining shelf life in days for the SKU as applied in this supply plan. Governs FEFO (First Expired First Out) rotation policy and constrains planned shipment sequencing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this supply plan record originated. Supports data lineage and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  supply_constraint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supply plan record reflects a constrained supply scenario where planned production or receipts are insufficient to fully satisfy planned demand. Triggers allocation decision workflows.']
  supply_demand_balance_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net supply-demand balance position for the SKU over the planning horizon (planned supply minus planned demand). Positive values indicate surplus; negative values indicate a supply gap requiring allocation decisions.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for all quantity fields in this supply plan record (e.g., CS=Case, EA=Each, KG=Kilogram). Aligns with the SKU\'s base UOM in SAP S/4HANA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|L|GAL|MT|OZ — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supply plan record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks revisions and supports audit trail for IBP governance.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential integer version number of this supply plan snapshot within a planning cycle. Increments with each revision, enabling plan-vs-plan variance analysis.']

  Note: 'Constrained supply plan defining planned production quantities, planned receipts, planned shipments, and supply-demand balance positions at SKU/plant/DC level across the planning horizon. Supports full version management with plan snapshots for cycle-over-cycle comparison, version numbering, creation timestamps, planning cycle references, planner attribution, approval workflows, and plan-vs-plan variance analysis. Drives replenishment signals, production scheduling, and allocation decisions when supply is constrained. Each plan instance captures approval status, key volume metrics (total planned production, total planned receipts), and maintains audit trail for IBP governance.'
}

  supplier_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_site.   actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which goods were physically received at the destination location (goods receipt posting date). Used to calculate On Time In Full (OTIF) performance, lead time actuals, and delivery variance against requested and confirmed dates.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'The Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) number transmitted by the supplier or source location confirming shipment of this replenishment order. Enables proactive receiving dock scheduling, WMS pre-receipt processing, and shipment tracking. Transmitted via EDI 856.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date confirmed by the supplier or supplying location. May differ from requested_delivery_date. Used to calculate delivery date variance, assess OTIF risk, and trigger escalation workflows when confirmation is later than requested.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity confirmed by the supplier or supplying location as available and committed for fulfillment. May differ from ordered_quantity due to partial availability, capacity constraints, or MOQ adjustments. Used for supply-demand gap analysis and OTIF measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this replenishment order record was first captured in the data platform. Distinct from order_date (business event time). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA monitoring of data freshness. Maps to RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit_cost and total_order_value are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency procurement and financial consolidation across global operations.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration or best-before date of the batch being replenished. Used to enforce FEFO rotation policy, calculate shelf_life_days_remaining, and ensure compliance with customer minimum remaining shelf life requirements. Critical for food safety and waste reduction.']
  ibp_plan_version varchar [note: 'The SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) plan version or S&OP cycle version under which this replenishment order was generated (e.g., \'BASELINE_2024Q3\', \'CONSENSUS_V2\'). Enables comparison of supply proposals across planning scenarios and IBP consensus cycles.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The planned lead time in calendar days from order release to expected goods receipt at the destination location. Derived from the purchasing info record or supply planning parameters. Used for replenishment timing calculations, safety stock parameter setting, and supply planning optimization.']
  moq_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ordered_quantity meets or exceeds the Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) requirement for this material-supplier combination. True = compliant; False = below MOQ threshold, requiring buyer review or rounding up before release.']
  moq_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) applicable to this replenishment order as defined in the purchasing info record or supply planning parameters. Ensures ordered_quantity meets supplier or logistics minimum thresholds. Critical for procurement compliance and cost optimization.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'The MRP controller code or planner identifier responsible for managing the replenishment of this material at the source plant. Used for workload assignment, exception management, and accountability tracking in supply planning operations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, buyer comments, exception explanations, or special handling instructions associated with this replenishment order. Used by supply planners and procurement teams to document non-standard situations, escalations, or supplier communications.']
  order_date date [note: 'The business date on which the replenishment order was created or generated by the MRP/supply planning system. Represents the principal real-world event time for this transaction. Used for aging analysis, lead time calculation, and OTIF measurement baseline. Maps to BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the replenishment order as assigned by the ERP or supply planning system (e.g., SAP purchase order number, stock transport order number, or MRP planned order number). Used for cross-system traceability and communication with suppliers and logistics partners. Maps to BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the replenishment order. Tracks progression from MRP-generated planned order through firming, release to warehouse/supplier, in-transit movement, and final goods receipt. Cancelled indicates the order was voided before fulfillment. Maps to LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the replenishment order by its procurement mechanism. Stock Transfer Order (STO) moves inventory between internal plants or DCs. Purchase Requisition is a request to procure from an external supplier. Planned Order is an MRP-generated supply proposal. Purchase Order is a confirmed external procurement. Intercompany Transfer moves goods between legal entities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stock_transfer_order|purchase_requisition|planned_order|purchase_order|intercompany_transfer — promote to reference product]']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of material requested in the replenishment order, expressed in the base unit of measure (e.g., cases, pallets, kg). This is the gross supply proposal quantity generated by MRP or supply planning before any MOQ rounding or adjustment.']
  planned_goods_issue_date date [note: 'The planned date on which goods are to be issued from the source location (outbound shipment departure). Used for outbound logistics scheduling, warehouse pick/pack planning, and lead time calculation between goods issue and goods receipt.']
  planning_run_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRP or supply planning run that generated this replenishment order. Enables traceability of supply proposals back to the specific planning cycle, demand signal, and parameter set used. Supports audit of planning decisions and scenario comparison.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the replenishment order to govern fulfillment sequencing when supply is constrained. Critical = immediate action required (e.g., stockout risk); High = elevated urgency; Medium = standard planning cycle; Low = opportunistic or forward-buy.']
  quality_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this replenishment order or the associated batch is subject to a quality hold that prevents goods receipt or putaway at the destination. True = quality hold active; False = no hold. Integrates with QM module and HACCP/GMP compliance processes.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual quantity of material received at the destination location upon goods receipt. Used to calculate fill rate, identify short shipments, and trigger OTIF performance measurement against the confirmed quantity.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the replenishment order is required to arrive at the destination location to meet demand or safety stock targets. Drives supplier scheduling, transportation booking, and OTIF measurement. Set by supply planning based on demand signals and inventory parameters.']
  rotation_policy varchar [note: 'The inventory rotation policy applicable to this replenishment order. FIFO (First In First Out) = oldest stock consumed first; FEFO (First Expired First Out) = stock with earliest expiry date consumed first (critical for perishable food and beverage products); LIFO (Last In First Out) = rarely used in food industry.']
  safety_stock_trigger boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this replenishment order was triggered by a safety stock breach (projected inventory falling below safety stock level) rather than a standard demand-driven replenishment signal. True = safety stock replenishment; False = demand-driven or scheduled replenishment.']
  shelf_life_days_remaining varchar [note: 'The number of days of remaining shelf life on the material at the time of order creation, based on the production date and expiry date of the batch being replenished. Critical for FEFO compliance, quality assurance, and ensuring product arrives at destination with sufficient remaining shelf life to meet customer requirements.']
  total_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of the replenishment order calculated as ordered_quantity multiplied by unit_cost. Used for procurement spend analysis, inventory investment tracking, and financial planning. Expressed in the order currency. Classified as confidential.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'The mode of transportation used to move the replenishment order from source to destination location. Drives freight cost estimation, lead time calculation, and carbon footprint reporting for sustainability compliance.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard or purchase cost per unit of measure for the material in this replenishment order. Used to calculate total order value, Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) impact, and inventory valuation. Classified as confidential as it reflects procurement pricing strategy.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure in which ordered, confirmed, and received quantities are expressed (e.g., CS=Cases, EA=Each, KG=Kilograms, LB=Pounds, PAL=Pallets, L=Liters, GAL=Gallons). Aligns with the material master UOM configuration in SAP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CS|EA|KG|LB|PAL|L|GAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this replenishment order record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks changes to order quantity, status, dates, or routing. Used for incremental data processing and audit compliance. Maps to RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']

  Note: 'Planned or released replenishment order generated by MRP or supply planning to move finished goods or raw materials between plants, DCs, and 3PL locations. Captures order type (stock transfer, purchase requisition, planned order), source and destination locations, requested quantity, MOQ compliance, priority, and order status lifecycle (planned → firmed → released → received). Foundation for inbound shipment creation and OTIF measurement.'
}

Table supply.safety_stock_policy {
  safety_stock_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the safety stock policy record. Primary key for the safety_stock_policy data product in the supply domain.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Safety‑stock policies are often set per brand to mitigate demand spikes generated by brand‑specific marketing promotions.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: In F&B, safety stock policies are elevated for specific marketing campaigns (is_promotional=true). Linking to the specific campaign that triggered the policy change enables campaign ROI analysis (incr']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Sales contracts with minimum service level commitments (e.g., 98.5% fill rate) directly govern safety stock policy parameters. Contract-driven inventory policy management is a named F&B process — plan']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Track safety‑stock holding costs against the cost center responsible for inventory.']
  finance_standard_cost_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_standard_cost. Business justification: Safety stock valuation: finance values safety stock inventory using standard costs; the policy defines the quantity, the standard cost record provides the unit value for balance sheet working capital ']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Safety stock policies are reviewed and approved within fiscal periods; finance uses safety stock levels to calculate working capital requirements per period for balance sheet planning. effective_date ']
  market_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market. Business justification: Safety stock policies in global F&B are market-specific — service_level_target_pct, lead_time_days, and demand_variability_factor differ by market. A domain expert expects safety_stock_policy to be ma']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the DC (Distribution Center) or warehouse location where this safety stock policy applies. Supports multi-node DC network and 3PL partner locations.']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Safety‑stock calculations must respect product‑specific regulatory limits (e.g., maximum allowable stock for allergen‑controlled items).']
  promotion_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: F&B supply planners create promotion-specific safety stock policies (is_promotional=true) tied to a specific promotion plan, setting elevated safety stock targets for SKUs during promotional windows. ']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: In F&B, safety stock policies are differentiated by customer segment — higher service level targets for key grocery accounts vs. foodservice or DTC. Linking segment_id enables segment-driven policy co']
  shelf_life_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.shelf_life_spec.
