  billing_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter country code of the billing address.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the billing address.']
  billing_state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the billing address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 code of the transaction currency.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be received.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert from transaction currency to reporting currency, if different.']
  freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Charges for transportation and logistics associated with the invoice.']
  invoice_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice was issued to the customer.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the invoice (e.g., sequential number).']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice purpose (standard, credit memo, debit memo, intercompany).']
  is_credit_memo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is a credit memo (true) or not (false).']
  line_count varchar [note: 'Number of line items included on the invoice.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used by the customer to settle the invoice.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment for this invoice.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual terms defining when payment is due (e.g., Net 30).']
  posted_to_ledger_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice has been posted to the general ledger.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Classification of revenue (e.g., gross sales, trade spend, net revenue).']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of line net amounts before discounts, freight, and tax.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax calculated for the invoice based on applicable tax codes.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction and rate applied.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied for tax calculation.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after all discounts, freight, and tax.']
  trade_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of trade promotions or discounts applied to the invoice.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the invoice record.']

  Note: 'Complete commercial invoice document issued to customers upon shipment or delivery of goods, capturing both header-level and line-level detail in a single authoritative billing record for revenue recognition. Header: invoice number, invoice date, invoice type (standard, credit memo, debit memo, intercompany), customer account, billing address, currency, subtotal, trade discount, freight, tax, total amount, payment terms, EDI 810 reference, and SAP SD billing document number. Lines: line number, SKU/GTIN, billed quantity, unit of measure, list price, invoice unit price, line discount, line net amount, tax code, cost center, profit center, GL account code, and revenue category (gross sales, trade spend, net revenue). SSOT for accounts receivable origination within sales domain; finance domain owns downstream AR aging and collections.'
}

Table sales.contract {
  contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sales contract.']
  account_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_hierarchy. Business justification: National account contracts in F&B (e.g., with Kroger, Walmart) apply across the full account hierarchy. Linking sales_contract to account_hierarchy enables contract compliance reporting, slotting fee ']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Contracts are negotiated per brand; linking enables brand‑level contract performance tracking and compliance reporting.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Links supply contracts to the distribution center fulfilling them, needed for contract performance and capacity planning reports.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Sales contracts in F&B are executed by a specific legal entity, determining applicable tax jurisdiction, currency, and statutory reporting obligations. Required for multi-entity F&B contract managemen']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Needed for Contract Signatory Register, linking each sales contract to the customer contact who authorized the agreement, required for compliance audit.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation of contract‑related expenses to a cost center is needed for budgeting, variance analysis, and cost‑to‑serve calculations.']
  label_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.label_approval. Business justification: F&B sales contracts — especially for private label, co-branded, or market-specific products — specify the approved label version the product must carry. Contract compliance audits and new product laun']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Contract compliance checks require linking each sales contract to the product registration to verify the product is authorized for sale.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Contracts are allocated to profit centers for performance tracking and internal profitability reporting; required by monthly P&L consolidation.']
  promotion_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_plan. Business justification: Contracts are often executed under a defined promotion plan; this link supports contract‑level promotion budgeting and performance tracking.']
  rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: A sales contract is negotiated and owned by a sales representative. Linking sales_contract.rep_id -> rep establishes accountability and enables rep-level contract portfolio analysis. No bidirectional ']
  retailer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer party associated with the contract.']
  retailer_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.retailer_agreement. Business justification: In F&B, sales contracts (slotting, volume commitments, EDLP) are executed under the framework of a retailer agreement. Contract management teams need to see which retailer agreement governs each sales']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: F&B sales contracts (EDLP, shelf-space, slotting agreements) are structured by customer segment (grocery, club, convenience). Linking contract to segment enables contract performance reporting and tra']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Contract management report ties each sales contract to a specific SKU for volume commitments and pricing; SKU ID is essential for accurate contract enforcement.']
  spec_quality_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Contract Specification Compliance: sales contracts reference agreed product specifications; linking ensures compliance monitoring and audit reporting.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: In F&B private label and co-pack arrangements, a retailer sales contract must reference the approved manufacturing supplier producing the goods. Regulatory traceability, retailer audits, and FSMA comp']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Sales contracts in F&B are often scoped to specific geographic territories (e.g., exclusive distribution agreements within a region). Linking sales_contract.territory_id -> territory formalizes this s']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews at expiration.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique contract number assigned by the company.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of the contract defining its primary purpose.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  edlp_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier for EDLP pricing structure.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract ends or expires; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes binding.']
  exclusive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the contract grants exclusive rights to the retailer.']
  fee_per_store decimal(10,2) [note: 'Slotting fee amount charged per store.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates if the contract is currently active (derived from status).']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when the product is scheduled to be launched in stores under this contract.']
  min_shelf_duration_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days the product must remain on shelf.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Terms governing payment schedule and method.']
  performance_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing velocity‑based performance criteria and penalties.']
  rebate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rebate applied based on volume thresholds.']
  rsp_commitment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Committed RSP per unit for the contract.']
  sales_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract.']
  slotting_fee_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate fee paid for shelf slotting across all stores.']
  store_count varchar [note: 'Number of retail locations covered by the contract.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days notice required to terminate the contract.']
  total_committed_volume decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total volume of product units committed under the contract.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the contract for amendment tracking.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the committed volume.']

  Note: 'Master commercial agreement between Food Beverage and a customer (retailer, foodservice operator, distributor) governing pricing, volume commitments, promotional terms, slotting fees, and service levels. Captures contract number, contract type (volume rebate, pricing agreement, distribution agreement, co-packing agreement, slotting agreement), effective dates, contracted volume, RSP commitments, EDLP pricing tiers, payment terms, exclusivity clauses, auto-renewal flag, and Salesforce CRM opportunity reference. For slotting-type contracts: includes retailer account, SKU/GTIN, store count, fee per store, total commitment, payment schedule, shelf section, launch date, minimum shelf duration, and velocity-based performance clauses. ADR: Slotting agreements are modeled as a contract_type within this product (not as a separate entity) because they are negotiated by sales/category teams during new item sell-in and directly tied to distribution point creation. Trade domain owns broader promotional agreements (TPM/TPO); this product owns the commercial commitment for shelf placement fees. Commission/incentive compensation calculation is owned by the finance/workforce domain; sales provides quota attainment data as input.'
}

Table sales.rep {
  rep_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for rep']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Sales reps in F&B are assigned to a cost center for compensation expense allocation and SG&A reporting. Finance tracks sales force costs by cost center for budget-vs-actual analysis and headcount cost']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: A sales rep is assigned to a primary territory — this is a core sales force management relationship. rep.territory_id -> territory normalizes the territory assignment. territory_code on rep is a denor']
  assigned_customer_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer accounts assigned to the rep.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of any required sales certifications.']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission percentage applied to sales.']
  compensation_plan varchar [note: 'Compensation structure applied to the rep.']
  compliance_training_completed_date date [note: 'Date when mandatory compliance training was completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rep record was created in the system.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address for the rep.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current employment status of the rep.']
  external_rep_code varchar [note: 'External identifier used by partners or third‑party systems.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the sales representative.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the rep was hired.']
  is_remote_worker boolean [note: 'Indicates if the rep works remotely.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communication with the rep.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent performance review.']
  next_performance_review_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next performance review.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the rep.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred primary contact channel for the rep.']
  quota_annual_cases decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target annual case volume quota.']
  quota_annual_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target annual revenue quota for the rep.']
  record_source varchar [note: 'Source system where the rep record originated.']
  rep_type varchar [note: 'Category of sales role performed by the rep.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel the rep operates in.']
  sales_district varchar [note: 'Specific sales district within the region.']
  sales_force_user_code varchar [note: 'User identifier in Salesforce CRM for the rep.']
  sales_region varchar [note: 'Geographic sales region assigned to the rep.']
  sap_employee_number varchar [note: 'Employee number used in SAP SD module.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the rep\'s employment ended, if applicable.']
  travel_region varchar [note: 'Travel region classification for the rep.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rep record.']

  Note: 'Sales force master record for field sales representatives, key account managers, and broker/agent personnel responsible for managing customer relationships and driving revenue. Captures rep ID, full name, rep type (field sales, key account manager, national account manager, broker, DSD driver), assigned territory, assigned customer accounts, sales region, sales district, quota (annual revenue and case volume), manager reference, employment status, Salesforce CRM user ID, and SAP SD sales employee number. SSOT for sales force identity within the sales domain.'
}

Table sales.territory {
  territory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for territory']
  market_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market. Business justification: Sales territories are aligned to marketing markets in F&B commercial planning (territory quota vs. marketing investment by market). This link enables integrated commercial planning reports comparing s']
  acv_coverage_target decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target ACV value (in USD) the territory aims to cover.']
  assigned_account_count varchar [note: 'Number of retail, foodservice, or DTC accounts assigned to the territory.']
  assigned_channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel(s) the territory serves: retail, foodservice, or direct‑to‑consumer.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the territory.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the territory record was first created in the system.']
  district varchar [note: 'Sub‑region within a region used for finer territory granularity.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the territory definition expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the territory definition becomes active.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the territory is exclusive to a single sales channel or partner.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent business review of the territory definition.']
  market_share_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired market share percentage for the territory.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the territory for resource allocation.']
  quota_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary sales quota assigned to the territory for the planning period.']
  sales_target_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected sales revenue target for the territory.']
  state_province_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of states or provinces included in the territory.']
  tdp_coverage_target varchar [note: 'Target number of distribution points the territory should reach.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the territory in sales and planning systems.']
  territory_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the territory.']
  territory_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the sales territory.']
  territory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the territory.']
  territory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the territory: geographic, account‑based, or channel‑based.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the territory record.']

  Note: 'Geographic and account-based sales territory definition used to assign sales reps, set quotas, and measure performance. Captures territory code, territory name, territory type (geographic, account-based, channel-based), region, district, country, state/province list, assigned channel (retail, foodservice, DTC), ACV (All Commodity Volume) coverage target, TDP (Total Distribution Points) target, number of assigned accounts, effective start and end dates, and territory manager reference. Enables territory planning, quota allocation, and sales performance measurement.'
}

Table sales.quota {
  quota_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the quota record.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: In F&B, sales quotas are derived from and reconciled against the revenue budget. Linking quota to the finance_budget record enables quota-vs-budget variance analysis and ensures sales targets align wi']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: F&B sales quotas are routinely set and tracked by product category (e.g., beverage quota vs. snack quota per rep/territory). This link supports category-level quota attainment reporting, a standard sa']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Sales quota-vs-actual revenue variance reporting in F&B aligns quotas to fiscal periods. Finance uses this link for management reporting and incentive compensation calculations. quota has text fiscal_']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative to whom the quota is assigned.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales territory or region for the quota assignment.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the quota was formally approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quota record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary quotas (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (1‑4) within the fiscal year for the quota.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the quota belongs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, comments, or audit notes related to the quota.']
  period_end date [note: 'Last calendar date of the quota period; nullable for open‑ended periods.']
  period_start date [note: 'First calendar date of the quota period (e.g., start of month, quarter, or year).']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the quota period (month, quarter, or year).']
  quota_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or special conditions of the quota.']
  quota_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the quota record.']
  quota_type varchar [note: 'Business dimension the quota targets (e.g., revenue, case volume, new distribution points, total distribution points, ACV, new SKU introductions).']
  region varchar [note: 'High‑level geographic region (e.g., NA, EMEA, APAC) associated with the quota.']
  source varchar [note: 'Origin of the quota allocation (top‑down corporate allocation, bottom‑up rep submission, or manual adjustment).']
  stretch_target_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Optional aggressive target above the primary quota to incentivize over‑performance.']
  target_confidence varchar [note: 'Confidence rating of the target based on forecasting methodology.']
  target_measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to derive the target (e.g., forecast, historical average, market share model).']
  target_units varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target value (e.g., USD, cases, points).']
  target_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Primary numeric target for the quota (e.g., $1,000,000 revenue or 200,000 cases).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the quota record.']

  Note: 'Sales quota assignment record defining revenue, volume, and distribution targets for a sales rep or territory over a defined period. Captures quota period (month, quarter, year), quota type (revenue, case volume, new distribution points, TDP), assigned rep or territory, target value, stretch target value, quota currency, quota status (draft, approved, active), approval date, approver reference, and quota source (top-down allocation, bottom-up submission). Enables sales performance management and incentive compensation calculation.'
}

Table sales.pos_transaction {
  pos_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the POS transaction record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Needed for POS Sales Attribution report, attributing each transaction to the retailer account for revenue and performance analysis.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: POS scan-level data must be linked to the driving campaign for marketing ROI measurement — calculating actual unit lift per campaign is a core F&B commercial analytics process. No existing campaign_id']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: POS transaction data in F&B is analyzed by product category for retail velocity, share-of-shelf, and category management reports. Replaces denormalized product_category and product_subcategory text co']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: POS transaction data is aggregated into fiscal periods for trade spend ROI analysis and sell-through revenue reporting in F&B. Finance reconciles POS-derived consumer offtake against invoiced shipment']
  market_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market. Business justification: POS transactions must be mapped to defined marketing markets (Nielsen/IRI DMAs) for market-share reporting. F&B commercial teams align scan data to marketing markets constantly. store_region is a deno']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_event. Business justification: POS scan data in F&B is the primary source for validating promotion compliance and measuring incremental lift. Trade analysts link scan transactions to promotion events for post-event ROI reporting an']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the product (SKU) sold.']
  store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.store. Business justification: POS scan data is captured at the retail store level. Linking pos_transaction.store_id -> store provides direct store-level traceability for consumer purchase activity. store_format and store_region on']
  average_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average price per unit for this line (dollar_sales / units_sold).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the POS record (retailer feed, syndicated provider, or direct partnership).']
  display_flag boolean [note: 'True if the product was featured on a shelf display at time of sale.']
  dollar_sales decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total sales amount in dollars for the line (units × price).']
  feature_flag boolean [note: 'True if the product was part of a special feature (e.g., end‑cap).']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the receipt.']
  month_of_year varchar [note: 'Month number (1‑12) for the transaction date.']
  pos_terminal_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal that captured the scan.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price applied (regular, promotional, discount).']
  promotion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the unit was sold under a promotional price.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this line to the parent receipt or transaction header.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first ingested into the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  retailer_banner varchar [note: 'Name of the retailer banner or chain (e.g., Walmart, Kroger).']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the sale was made.']
  scan_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the barcode was scanned by the terminal.']
  store_format varchar [note: 'Classification of the store format.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the transaction (store local time).']
  transaction_day_of_week varchar [note: 'Day of week on which the transaction occurred. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Mon|Tue|Wed|Thu|Fri|Sat|Sun — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transaction_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business‑level identifier for the transaction (may be receipt number).']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the transaction line.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the transaction was recorded.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the transaction line (sale, return, or inventory adjustment).']
  transaction_week varchar [note: 'Week number (1‑52) derived from transaction_date.']
  units_sold varchar [note: 'Number of units of the product scanned in this line.']
  week_of_year varchar [note: 'ISO week number for the transaction date.']
  year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit year of the transaction.']

  Note: 'Point-of-sale scan data record capturing consumer purchase activity at the retail store level, sourced from retailer POS feeds, syndicated data providers (Nielsen/IRI/Circana/SPINS), and direct retailer data sharing programs (e.g., Walmart Luminate, Kroger 84.51°). Captures store ID, retailer banner, UPC/GTIN, transaction date/week, units sold, dollar sales, average retail price, promoted/display/feature flags, and data source. SSOT for raw/ingested POS scan data within the enterprise; analytics and insights domains consume this data for derived metrics (market share, promotional lift, price elasticity) but do not own the source records. Enables sell-through velocity analysis, promotional lift measurement, share tracking, and category management insights at the store-week-SKU grain.'
}

Table sales.distribution_point {
  distribution_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the distribution point record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Needed for Distribution Planning Dashboard, associating each distribution point with the retailer account it serves to allocate inventory.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Distribution points capture presence of each brand; linking allows brand distribution metrics and market coverage reporting.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: TDP (Total Distribution Points) gains are a primary KPI for campaign effectiveness in F&B. Linking distribution_point to campaign enables measurement of campaign-driven distribution expansion — a stan']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_center. Business justification: Supports Distribution Point Allocation report linking retailer points to the supplying distribution center for inventory planning.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Distribution point snapshots in F&B are aligned to fiscal periods for trade spend ROI and distribution KPI reporting. Finance uses numeric/ACV distribution metrics by fiscal period to evaluate trade i']
  market_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market. Business justification: Distribution points are measured within defined marketing markets for ACV-weighted distribution reporting aligned to syndicated data (Nielsen/IRI). market_area is a denormalized plain-text descriptor ']
  product_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_registration. Business justification: Distribution planning uses product registration data to ensure only compliant products are stocked at each point.']
  recall_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.recall_event. Business justification: When an F&B recall is initiated, distribution points stocking the affected product must be immediately identified for scope determination and retailer notification. Recall effectiveness checks and FDA']
  retailer_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.retailer_agreement. Business justification: Retailer agreements in F&B include TDP (Total Distribution Points) and numeric distribution commitments. Agreement compliance reporting requires matching actual distribution_point snapshots against th']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Distribution analytics require linking each distribution point record to the exact SKU to track inventory, velocity, and shelf‑life per product.']
  store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.store. Business justification: A distribution point represents SKU-level distribution presence at a specific retail store. Linking distribution_point.store_id -> store connects the distribution metric snapshot to the store master r']
  acv_change_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percent change in ACV weighted distribution versus prior period.']
  acv_weighted_distribution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ACV represented by outlets where the SKU is active, weighted by sales volume.']
  classification_type varchar [note: 'Category of outlet: retail store, foodservice location, or direct‑to‑consumer channel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the distribution point record was first created in the system.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the syndicated data snapshot (Nielsen, IRI, or Circana).']
  distribution_point_code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned code uniquely identifying the distribution point within the organization.']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the SKU at the outlet: authorized (contracted), active (selling), or voided (no longer carried).']
  in_stock_flag boolean [note: 'True if the SKU is currently stocked at the outlet; false otherwise.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the outlet or distribution point.']
  numeric_distribution_change_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percent change in numeric distribution versus prior period.']
  numeric_distribution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total outlets carrying the SKU, regardless of sales weight.']
  period_over_period_change_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percent change of the primary metric compared to the previous period.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Aggregation window of the snapshot: weekly, 4‑week, 13‑week, or 52‑week.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the record itself: active, inactive, or archived.']
  retailer_banner varchar [note: 'Brand or banner under which the outlet operates (e.g., Walmart, Kroger, Starbucks).']
  snapshot_date date [note: 'Date of the syndicated data snapshot (weekly, 4‑week, etc.).']
  tdp_change_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percent change in TDP contribution versus prior period.']
  tdp_contribution_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Share of total distribution points contributed by the outlet for the SKU.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the distribution point record.']
  velocity_units_per_point decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average units sold per distribution point for the SKU over the snapshot period.']
  void_date date [note: 'Date on which the SKU distribution was terminated at the outlet.']

  Note: 'SSOT for SKU-level distribution presence, measurement, and periodic syndicated metric snapshots at retail and foodservice locations. Captures store/outlet ID, retailer banner, SKU/GTIN, distribution status (authorized, active, voided), ACV weighted distribution percentage, TDP (Total Distribution Points) contribution, numeric distribution percentage, velocity (units per point of distribution), in-stock status, void date, and periodic snapshot data by time period (weekly, 4-week, 13-week, 52-week) including snapshot date, market area, data source (Nielsen/IRI/Circana), and period-over-period change metrics. Enables distribution health monitoring, new item launch tracking, void management, TDP target attainment reporting, ACV trend analysis, and syndicated data reconciliation. Subsumes all periodic ACV/TDP snapshot reporting previously modeled separately.'
}

  Note: 'Master record for food and beverage sales brokers and manufacturer representatives who sell Food Beverage products on behalf of the company in specific channels or geographies. Captures broker ID, broker company name, broker type (retail broker, foodservice broker, DSD broker, export agent), coverage territory, covered channels, covered customer accounts, commission rate structure, commission basis (gross sales, net sales, cases), contract effective dates, performance tier, primary contact, and SAP vendor number. SSOT for broker identity and commercial terms within the sales domain; customer domain owns the broader customer/partner entity but sales owns the broker-as-selling-agent relationship, commission structures, and performance management. Enables broker management, commission calculation, and channel coverage governance.'
}

This link drives service-level assignment, lead-time calculation, and distribution network ']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: In F&B, stores are classified into customer segments (e.g., premium grocery, value, convenience) to drive targeted assortment, planogram, and promotional execution decisions. Linking store to segment ']
  ship_to_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.ship_to_location. Resolving store to ship_to_location is ']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: In DSD (Direct Store Delivery) and F&B retail operations, each store has an associated back-room or in-store storage location. This link enables store-level replenishment planning, DSD inventory manag']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: A retail store belongs to a sales territory for field sales coverage, quota assignment, and distribution tracking. Linking store.territory_id -> territory connects the store master to the territory ma']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the store.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for store. Referenced by store_id.'

Table sales.contract_store_coverage {
  contract_store_coverage_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for this contract-store coverage record.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the master sales contract that governs this store coverage.']
  store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific store covered under this contract.']
  coverage_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this store\'s coverage under the contract (active, pending, suspended, terminated). Allows per-store status management independent of the master contract status.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contract-store coverage record was created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this specific store\'s coverage under this contract ends. This attribute was identified in the detection phase and allows per-store coverage dates that may differ from the master contract dates.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this specific store became covered under this contract. This attribute was identified in the detection phase and allows per-store coverage dates that may differ from the master contract dates.']
  fee_per_store decimal(10,2) [note: 'Slotting fee or other per-store financial obligation for this specific store under this contract. This attribute was identified in the detection phase and currently exists on sales_contract but logically belongs to the per-store relationship.']
  min_shelf_duration_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days the product must remain on shelf at this specific store under this contract. This attribute was identified in the detection phase and currently exists on sales_contract but logically belongs to the per-store relationship.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Contract between sales_contract and store. It captures the specific stores covered under a master sales contract (chain-wide slotting, EDLP, or distribution agreement) and the per-store financial and operational terms. Each record links one sales_contract to one store with attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship, such as per-store fees, effective dates, and minimum shelf duration commitments.. Existence Justification: In Food & Beverage retail, master sales contracts (chain-wide slotting agreements, EDLP pricing agreements, distribution agreements) routinely cover multiple stores within a retail chain, and each store participates in multiple contracts simultaneously (e.g., a store may be covered by a slotting agreement for Brand A, an EDLP agreement for Brand B, and a promotional agreement for Brand C). The business actively manages contract-store coverage as a recognized operational entity in trade spend management systems, tracking per-store fees, coverage dates, and shelf duration commitments.'
}

Table trade.promotion_plan {
  promotion_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the promotion plan record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand‑level promotion planning uses this FK to allocate spend and track performance per brand.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Integrated trade-and-marketing planning: F&B trade planners build promotion plans in direct alignment with marketing campaigns (e.g., summer snack campaign drives retailer promotion plan). Brand manag']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: Trade spend planning in F&B is structured by product category (e.g., \'Snacks Q3 promotion\'). Category-level promotion plan scoping drives trade spend allocation reports, category management reviews, a']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Promotion plans operate within legal entity boundaries; company code determines chart of accounts, fiscal calendar, and currency for plan budgeting and GL posting rules.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Plan effective dates must align with fiscal periods for budget allocation, accrual timing, and financial statement reporting of promotional investments.']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.fund. Business justification: Promotion plans allocate financial resources from funds; linking promotion_plan to fund enables budget tracking.']
  primary_promotion_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer account targeted by the promotion.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Promotion plans are often co‑funded by suppliers; the Promotion Planning process records the responsible supplier for budgeting and ROI analysis.']
  target_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Segment-Based Promotion Planning: F&B trade marketers design promotion plans targeting specific customer segments (e.g., value channel vs. premium channel). This link enables segment-level ROI reporti']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the promotion plan.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the promotion plan received final approval.']
  baseline_volume varchar [note: 'Projected sales volume without the promotion (historical baseline).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel targeted by the promotion.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the promotion plan record was created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date when the promotion expires.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date when the promotion becomes active.']
  estimated_roi_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected return on investment expressed as a percentage.']
  forecast_created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the analyst who created the forecast.']
  forecast_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the forecast was generated.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'Method used to generate the promotion forecast.']
  forecast_version varchar [note: 'Identifier for the version of the forecast model or scenario.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the financial resources allocated to the promotion.']
  incremental_lift_estimate varchar [note: 'Estimated additional units sold due to the promotion.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion is exclusive to the retailer (true) or shared with other retailers (false).']
  net_revenue_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected net revenue change after accounting for trade spend and incremental sales.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions for the promotion.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'External code used by trade marketing to reference the promotion plan.']
  promotion_mechanic varchar [note: 'Mechanic used to deliver the promotion (e.g., discount, rebate, free gift).']
  promotion_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the promotion plan for reporting and UI display.']
  promotion_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the promotion plan.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Category of promotional event (e.g., temporary price reduction, display, BOGO).']
  promotional_budget_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary fields in the promotion plan.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market where the promotion is executed (e.g., NA, EU).']
  sku_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of SKU identifiers participating in the promotion.']
  target_sales_units varchar [note: 'Sales unit target set for the promotion period.']
  target_sales_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary sales target (e.g., USD) for the promotion.']
  total_promoted_volume varchar [note: 'Sum of baseline and incremental lift – total units expected to be sold under the promotion.']
  trade_spend_investment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount allocated to the promotion (discounts, rebates, fees).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the promotion plan.']

  Note: 'Master record for trade promotion plans created by sales/trade marketing teams. Defines the complete promotional event including promotion type (TPR, display, feature ad, BOGO, scan-back), planned start/end dates, target retailer accounts, participating SKUs, funding source, promotional mechanic, approval status, and volume/ROI forecast. Includes planning-stage forecasts: baseline volume, incremental lift estimate, total promoted volume, net revenue impact, trade spend investment, estimated ROI, forecast method (statistical model, historical analogy, sales rep input), and forecast version. Serves as the authoritative header for all downstream execution, accrual, deduction, and settlement tracking. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD, TPM modules, and SAP IBP demand sensing.'
}

Table trade.promotion_event {
  promotion_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotion event record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand-level trade spend reporting: F&B brand managers track all promotion events for their brand (e.g., all Lay\'s display events). Direct brand_id on promotion_event enables brand P&L reporting withou']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for Campaign‑Promotion ROI report linking each promotion event to its originating marketing campaign.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: promotion_event.product_category is a denormalized plain-text field. F&B trade execution tracks promotion events by product category for post-event ROI analysis and category-level compliance reporting']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Events execute within specific legal entities; company code determines GL posting rules, intercompany treatment, and tax jurisdiction for promotional spend.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Promotion expense must be posted to a Cost Center for budgeting and P&L reporting; finance cost_center tracks these allocations.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Event actual dates must map to fiscal periods for revenue recognition, accrual posting, and period-end close reconciliation of promotional spend.']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.fund. Business justification: A promotion event draws spend from a trade fund. While the path promotion_event -> promotion_plan -> fund exists, events may be funded by a different fund than the plan (e.g., co-op funds vs. trade fu']
  market_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.market. Business justification: Regional trade spend analysis: F&B promotion events are executed in specific geographic markets; market-level reporting (e.g., Northeast US vs. Southeast US display events) is a core trade analytics n']
  promotion_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent promotion plan.']
  retailer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer account where the promotion was executed.']
  retailer_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.retailer_agreement. Business justification: A promotion event executes under the terms of a retailer agreement (JBP, scan-back rates, compliance requirements). Linking promotion_event directly to retailer_agreement enables compliance tracking, ']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual end date when the promotion concluded at the retailer.']
  actual_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual revenue generated by the promotion.']
  actual_sales_volume varchar [note: 'Actual sales volume achieved during the promotion.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual start date when the promotion began at the retailer.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion event record was approved by audit.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Internal audit status of the promotion event record.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel(s) where the promotion was applied.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding compliance observations.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance assessment result for the promotion execution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion event record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business reference number for the promotion event.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion event was actually executed in the field.']
  field_verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the field verification.']
  is_field_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether field execution was verified by a supervisor.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the promotion event record.']
  mechanic_type varchar [note: 'Type of promotional mechanic deployed for the event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes about the promotion event.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date of the promotion as per the promotion plan.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the promotion as per the promotion plan.']
  promotion_event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotion event.']
  promotion_objective varchar [note: 'Primary business objective of the promotion.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Category of promotion type applied.']
  promotional_materials_delivered boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if promotional materials were delivered to the store.']
  promotional_materials_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of promotional materials delivered.']
  promotional_materials_type varchar [note: 'Type of promotional materials used for the event.']
  promotional_spend_discount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total discount or chargeback amount applied to the promotional spend.']
  promotional_spend_gross decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gross promotional spend amount before any deductions.']
  promotional_spend_net decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net promotional spend after discounts and chargebacks.']
  sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SKUs included in the promotion.']
  store_count_actual varchar [note: 'Number of stores that actually executed the promotion.']
  store_count_planned varchar [note: 'Number of stores originally scheduled to participate in the promotion.']
  target_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned revenue target for the promotion.']
  target_sales_volume varchar [note: 'Planned sales volume target (units) for the promotion.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the promotion event record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the execution of a trade promotion event at a specific retailer account and store cluster. Tracks actual execution dates, participating store count, promotional mechanic deployed (e.g., end-cap display, circular feature, TPR shelf tag), compliance status, and field execution confirmation. Links to the parent promotion plan and captures variance between planned and actual execution. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud and field execution data.'
}

Table trade.promotion_line {

Currently promotion_lin']
  reservation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.reservation. Business justification: Promotional volume commitments require inventory reservation to guarantee stock availability for the event. F&B trade execution process: promotion_line drives a reservation at DC/plant level to preven']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Promotion line records financial impact per SKU; adding SKU FK enables detailed analysis.']
  spend_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.spend_budget. Business justification: promotion_line has budget_amount, total_funding_amount, and funding_amount_per_unit — these represent draws against a spend budget. Linking promotion_line to spend_budget enables line-level budget con']
  target_customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: REQUIRED: Promotion lines are built to target specific customer segments; the segment ID is needed for segment‑based promotion planning reports.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate promotion funding across SKUs.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the promotion line.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion line was approved or rejected.']
  baseline_volume decimal(12,2) [note: 'Expected sales volume without the promotion.']
  bill_back_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Rate used for bill‑back calculations on promotional sales.']
  budget_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated for the promotion line.']
  cost_of_goods_sold_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'COGS for the SKU used to calculate margin on promotional sales.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion ends for this line.']

  incremental_volume_target decimal(12,2) [note: 'Targeted additional units to be sold beyond baseline.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion is exclusive to the retailer.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the promotion.']
  margin_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Profit margin per unit after accounting for promotion funding.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks or instructions.']
  off_invoice_allowance_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Rate of off‑invoice allowance expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.10 = 10%).']
  planned_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Planned promotional price per unit for the SKU.']
  planned_volume_lift decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected increase in units sold due to the promotion.']
  promotion_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the promotion strategy.']
  promotion_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotion line.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism of the promotion applied to the SKU.']

  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code for the promotion line.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the promotion is offered.']
  scan_back_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Rate applied to retailer‑scanned sales for reimbursement.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion becomes effective for this line.']
  target_store_type varchar [note: 'Type of store (e.g., supermarket, convenience) targeted by the promotion.']
  total_funding_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total monetary funding for the promotion line (derived from volume and per‑unit funding).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the promotion line record.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail within a trade promotion plan representing a specific SKU or product group included in the promotion. Captures planned promotional price, off-invoice allowance rate, scan-back rate, bill-back rate, planned volume lift, baseline volume, incremental volume target, and funding amount per unit. Enables SKU-level trade spend allocation and ROI measurement. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD condition records and TPM line detail.'
}

Table trade.fund {
  fund_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each trade fund record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Customer Fund Allocation: F&B trade funds are frequently designated to specific retailer accounts (e.g., a Kroger-specific co-op fund). This link enables fund utilization reporting by account and ensu']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Trade fund allocation is performed per brand; this FK enables fund‑to‑brand reconciliation reports.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: Trade funds in F&B are allocated by product category (e.g., \'Beverages MDF fund\', \'Snacks co-op fund\'). Category-scoped fund allocation is required for trade spend governance, fund utilization reporti']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Trade funds are legal-entity-specific; company code controls fund currency, GL account assignment, and intercompany allocation rules for promotional budgets.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Trade funds are allocated to specific Profit Centers for performance measurement and internal reporting.']
  accrued_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount accrued based on spend that has been recognized but not yet paid.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to distribute the fund across eligible promotions.']
  allocation_scope varchar [note: 'Level at which the fund is allocated (e.g., retailer, sales territory, brand/category).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the fund was approved by finance or trade management.']
  classification varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the fund purpose (e.g., marketing, price promotion, slotting).']
  committed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of amounts already committed to promotion plans.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fund record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the fund amounts.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the fund becomes effective for spend.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the fund expires or is no longer available (nullable for open‑ended).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the fund belongs.']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the trade fund in external systems and contracts.']
  fund_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context or notes about the fund.']
  fund_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the trade fund for reporting and analysis.']
  fund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fund.']
  fund_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the fund is a lump‑sum, accrual‑based, or fixed allowance allocation.']
  paid_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount that has been paid out to retailers or partners.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the geographic region for the fund allocation.']
  remaining_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining budget available for future commitments.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the fund data (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Oracle ERP).']
  total_approved_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budget approved for the fund, expressed in the fund currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fund record.']

  Note: 'Master record for trade spending funds and budgets allocated to retailer accounts, sales territories, or brand/category buckets. Tracks fund type (lump sum, accrual-based, fixed allowance), total approved budget, amount committed to promotion plans, amount accrued, amount paid, and remaining balance. Supports trade spend governance and budget control. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA CO and TPM fund management modules.'
}

Table trade.accrual {
  accrual_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the accrual record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retailer with which the promotion is executed.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Accruals post to GL within company code; determines posting rules, intercompany elimination, and legal entity financial statement impact.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Accruals allocated to cost centers for P&L reporting, budget variance analysis, and sales region/brand team accountability tracking.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Accruals are period-specific for financial statement timing, reversal logic, and matching promotional expense to revenue period.']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.fund. Business justification: Accruals represent financial liabilities recognized as products are sold under trade promotions — they directly reduce fund balances. A direct FK from accrual to fund is essential for fund balance rec']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Accrual posting requires specific GL account (liability or expense) for month-end close, financial statement classification, and audit trail.']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional agreement that generated this accrual.']
  retailer_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.retailer_agreement. Business justification: Accruals are generated under the terms of retailer agreements (e.g., scan-back rates, bill-back rates defined in agreement_term). Linking accrual directly to retailer_agreement enables agreement-level']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Financial accruals must be tied to a specific SKU for GAAP reporting; linking ensures accurate cost allocation per product.']
  spend_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.spend_budget. Business justification: Accruals consume spend budget allocations. Linking accrual to spend_budget enables real-time budget consumption tracking (accrued_amount on spend_budget is the rollup of individual accruals). This FK ']
  accrual_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the accrual, used in reporting and audit trails.']
  accrual_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accrual record.']
  accrual_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the accrual is calculated (e.g., off‑invoice, bill‑back, scan‑back, lump‑sum).']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the accrual liability recognized for the period.']
  basis varchar [note: 'Basis used to compute the accrual (per case, per unit, or percentage of net sales).']
  basis_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity (cases, units, etc.) used as the denominator for the accrual calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the accrual record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the accrual amount.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the accrual becomes effective for accounting purposes.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the accrual liability expires or is no longer valid.']
  net_sales_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net sales value for the period, used when the accrual basis is a percentage of net sales.']
  period_end date [note: 'Last calendar date of the period for which the accrual is calculated.']
  period_start date [note: 'First calendar date of the period for which the accrual is calculated.']
  posting_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the GL posting transaction generated from this accrual.']
  rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Rate applied to the accrual basis (e.g., $0.25 per case or 2.5% of net sales).']
  reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description explaining the business rationale for the accrual.']
  recognition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the accrual was recognized in the financial system.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'True if the accrual has been reversed or cancelled; otherwise False.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the accrual record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing trade promotion accruals — the financial liability recognized as products are sold under a promotional agreement. Tracks accrual type (off-invoice, bill-back, scan-back, lump sum), accrual rate, accrual basis (per case, per unit, percentage of net sales), accrual period, accrued amount, reversal status, and GL posting reference. Critical for accurate P&L and COGS reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO accrual engine.'
}

Table trade.deduction {
  deduction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the trade deduction record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the retail customer or distributor that submitted the deduction claim.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Deductions post to AR/GL within company code; determines clearing workflow, dispute resolution authority, and legal entity revenue impact.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Deduction date must map to fiscal period for revenue adjustment, aging analysis, and period-end AR reconciliation.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Deduction amounts must be posted to a GL Account to reflect chargebacks and adjustments in the general ledger.']
  goods_movement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.goods_movement. Business justification: Short-shipment and over-shipment deductions are directly triggered by specific goods movements. F&B deduction resolution process requires linking the deduction to the causative goods movement document']
  ingredient_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ingredient.lot. Business justification: Quality-driven deduction resolution: F&B retailers deduct for products tied to specific ingredient lots (contamination, spec failure). Linking deduction to ingredient.lot enables root-cause traceabili']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice against which the deduction is applied.']
  non_conformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.non_conformance. Business justification: Retailer deductions in F&B are frequently driven by quality non-conformances (defective product, short weight, labeling errors). Linking deductions to the originating NCR is essential for root cause a']
  product_recall_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.product_recall. Business justification: Retailers take deductions against invoices for recalled product returns and destruction costs. Trade finance teams must link recall-related deductions to the product recall record for financial reconc']
  promotion_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to trade.promotion_claim. Business justification: In trade promotion management, retailers take deductions against invoices and submit promotion claims to justify them. Linking deduction to promotion_claim enables deduction-to-claim matching, which i']
  promotion_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotion linked to the deduction.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: In F&B, retailer deductions are frequently tied to originating purchase orders for deduction reconciliation and chargeback dispute resolution. purchase_order_number (plain text) is a denormalized sign']
  recall_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.recall_event. Business justification: Recall-related deductions (product returns, disposal costs, shelf withdrawal fees) must be tracked against specific recall events for cost recovery, insurance claims, and financial impact analysis. Es']
  retailer_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade agreement or contract governing the deduction terms.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Chargeback deductions are calculated per SKU; FK enables traceability of deductions to the exact product for audit and rebate reconciliation.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of adjustments (e.g., discounts, fees) applied to the gross amount.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the deduction was approved.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit log entry capturing change history for this deduction.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for the product lot involved in the deduction.']
  chargeback_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied for chargeback calculations.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by users.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the deduction record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the deduction amounts.']
  deduction_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category classifying the nature of the deduction.']
  deduction_date date [note: 'Date the deduction was recorded or became effective.']
  deduction_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the deduction.']
  deduction_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the deduction by the retailer or distributor.']
  deduction_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the deduction.']
  deduction_type varchar [note: 'Category describing why the deduction was recorded.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the deduction is currently under dispute.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the deduction is disputed.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the product lot expires, relevant for quality‑related deductions.']
  freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Freight cost component related to the deduction.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original amount claimed before any adjustments or offsets.']
  handling_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Handling fee component associated with the deduction.']
  is_manual_entry boolean [note: 'True if the deduction was entered manually rather than via automated feed.']
  market_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment of the retailer or distributor.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after adjustments, representing the amount to be deducted from payment.']
  processing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the deduction was processed in the downstream system.']
  quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of product units associated with the deduction.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for the deduction.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code of the geographic region where the deduction originated.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system that created the deduction record.']
  supporting_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to external documentation (e.g., claim form) that supports the deduction.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the deduction, if applicable.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction or rule applied.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity field.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the deduction record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for all trade-related deductions taken against invoices by retail customers and distributors — including short payments, promotional claims, chargebacks reconciling contracted vs. list price differences, and unauthorized deductions requiring resolution.'
}

Table trade.retailer_agreement {
  retailer_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the retailer agreement record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand-level trade contract management: In F&B, retailer agreements are frequently brand-specific (e.g., a Gatorade shelf placement agreement, a Doritos feature commitment). Brand managers need visibil']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: retailer_agreement.category_scope is a denormalized plain-text field. F&B retailer agreements are scoped to product categories (category captain agreements, category-wide EDLP commitments). FK to prod']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Agreements are legal contracts signed by specific legal entity (company code); determines contractual authority, payment terms, and intercompany billing.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: REQUIRED: Retailer agreements must record the primary retailer contact for contract execution and compliance; linking to contact provides the needed reference.']
  food_safety_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.food_safety_audit. Business justification: Retailer trading agreements in F&B routinely require current food safety certifications (BRC, SQF, FSSC) as a compliance condition. Trade compliance managers must link the qualifying audit to the agre']
  gfsi_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.gfsi_certification. Business justification: Major retailers mandate GFSI certification (BRC, SQF, FSSC 22000) as supplier qualification criteria in agreements. Links contractual quality requirements to certification status for supplier eligibil']
  primary_retailer_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Key account retailer agreements in F&B designate specific DCs or warehouses as sourcing locations (dedicated supply, EDLP commitments, slotting arrangements). Logistics compliance reporting and agreem']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the retailer agreement for easy reference.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the agreement (e.g., contract number).']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the agreement defining the primary commercial mechanism.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement was approved.']
  bonus_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary bonus awarded for exceeding performance thresholds.']
  bonus_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing bonuses for exceeding performance thresholds.']
  co_op_advertising_allowance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary allowance for cooperative advertising activities.']
  compliance_measure_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify retailer compliance with agreement terms.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary fields.']

  edlp_price_commitment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Committed price for Everyday Low Price (EDLP) items.']
  edlp_price_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the EDLP price commitment.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date the agreement expires or is terminated (nullable for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date the agreement becomes binding.']
  funding_commitment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount the retailer commits to fund promotional activities.']
  is_exclusive_agreement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the retailer is prohibited from similar agreements with competitors.']
  is_multi_year boolean [note: 'True if the agreement spans multiple fiscal years.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the agreement record.']
  mdf_allocation_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Allocated amount for Marketing Development Funds (MDF).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or special conditions.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty applied when performance thresholds are not met.']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing penalties for non‑performance.']
  performance_measure_period varchar [note: 'Frequency at which performance is evaluated.']
  performance_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum performance metric (e.g., sales %) required to avoid penalties.']
  promotional_event_count varchar [note: 'Number of promotional events scheduled under the agreement.']
  rebate_tier_structure varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded definition of volume rebate tiers and rates.']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes an automatic renewal option.']
  retailer_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the agreement.']
  scan_back_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sales that must be reported back via scan‑back.']
  scan_back_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the scan‑back rate.']
  sku_scope varchar [note: 'List of specific SKUs covered by the agreement (comma‑separated).']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to give notice before terminating the agreement.']
  total_funding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary amount committed for promotional funding under the agreement.']
  volume_target decimal(12,2) [note: 'Committed sales volume target for the retailer (e.g., cases, units).']
  volume_target_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the volume target.']

  Note: 'Master record for formal trade agreements with retail customers including annual joint business plans (JBPs), volume rebate agreements, promotional calendars, EDLP pricing commitments, and category captain agreements. Captures agreement type, effective dates, retailer account, committed volume targets, promotional event count, funding commitments, performance thresholds, and penalty/bonus clauses. Contains granular line-item terms as child records: each term specifies type (volume rebate tier, promotional funding rate, EDLP price commitment, display compliance requirement, scan-back rate, co-op advertising allowance, MDF allocation), applicable SKU or category scope, threshold conditions, rate or amount, measurement period, and compliance measurement method. Supports multi-term agreement governance, compliance monitoring, and earned income calculation. SSOT for all retailer commercial terms. Sourced from Salesforce CRM and SAP S/4HANA SD contracts.'
}

Table trade.agreement_term {
